songs.subject: slow sunday in love, neo-soul
songs.lyrics:
Sunlight slipping through the blinds like honey,
Your hand on mine, everything feels sunny,
Coffee steam drawing soft little curls,
In this quiet corner of the world.
No rush, no need to be anywhere,
Just your breath tangled up in the air,
Records spin slow like a memory,
Of how easy love can be.

It’s a slow Sunday morning in your arms,
No alarms, just the warmth of your calm,
We’re floating where the daylight leans in,
Letting time forget where it’s been.
Every second feels like a soft refrain,
Like a song that never needs to change,
Just you and me in this gentle glow,
Moving as slow as the love we know.

Window open, breeze in the kitchen light,
You laugh like everything’s alright,
Bare feet dancing on the wooden floor,
I don’t need anything more.
Your voice is a low and steady groove,
Pulling me into your easy mood,
And the world outside can wait its turn,
While in here we let it burn slow.

If all the noise ever comes back again,
We’ll find this moment and start it then,
Right here where the morning feels like home,
And we’re never alone.

songs.subject: park chess hustler, boom-bap
songs.lyrics:
Park bench prophet, hoodie up, thoughts in a foggy optic,
Board on the table, he stable but barely got logic,
Sixteen, seen kings fall to his pawn shop tactics,
Knights get boxed quick, bishops get lost in the static.
Streetlight chess clock, no tick-tock, just talk slick,
Old heads step up, he dust ’em with a quick trick,
“Kid, you ain’t ready,” they said with a grin thin,
Next thing they know, he’s checkmating in ten pins.
He learned patience from silence, violence in timing,
Every move like a line in a jazz-band rhyming,
Queens get tempted, then sent to their endgame,
He plays like Miles—no rush, just flame.
Backpack full of paperbacks, diagrams, faded maps,
Studying gambits while other kids chase fading caps,
But he chasing titles in parks with a cracked board,
Where every loss is a lesson, every win is a fact stored.

In the park where the nights get deep,
He’s playing kings while the city won’t sleep,
One move, two moves, three moves gone,
Turned sidewalk stones into a chessboard song.
They came for a game, but they left with doubt,
’Cause the kid in the hoodie just figured it out,
From pawn to power in the lamplight glow,
That’s how a hustler’s legend starts to grow.

songs.subject: quiet marriage, country
songs.lyrics:
The porch light hums like it’s tired too,
Same two rocking chairs, same worn-out view,
We pass like smoke in a still-lit room,
Too careful not to collide.
Your coffee’s cold by the morning mail,
My words fall short like a half-told tale,
We’ve learned every silence too well,
And neither of us asks why.

It’s a quiet kind of breaking, slow and sure,
Like a house that forgets what it was built for,
We still share the bed but not the night,
Two shadows just doing time.
No storms left here, just fading sound,
Of two hearts that don’t talk out loud,
And I can’t tell if we’re still in love,
Or just afraid to let it go.

The radio plays what we used to sing,
But neither of us hums anything,
Your hand on mine feels like a ring
That’s lost its shape and shine.
We count the years in dishes and bills,
In the way the kitchen never feels still,
Like something waiting for a will
Neither of us can sign.

If I said your name like it used to be,
Would it echo back or just disappear to sea,
In the space between you and me,
Where the truth won’t come to stay?

songs.subject: surviving hard year, arena-rock
songs.lyrics:
I wore the weight of a hundred storms,
Walked through fire in borrowed forms,
Every sunrise felt like a test I’d fail,
Every night like a setting sail.
I lost my footing, I lost my name,
Got cut by doubt but I stayed in the game,
Bit down hard on the edge of fear,
But I made it through—I’m still here.

And I’m standing in the wreckage singing loud,
Heart like thunder, head above the crowd,
Yeah, I broke but I rebuilt every piece,
Turned my scars into release.
This is my battle cry in the light of the flame,
I survived every shadow that called my name,
From the ashes I rise, unchained and free,
Nothing left that can bury me.

I learned the sound of my own grit,
When every loss tried to make me quit,
But I hit back harder with every fall,
Started climbing over every wall.
The mirrors cracked but I looked right in,
Saw the fight underneath my skin,
And I swore to the ghost of who I’d been,
I would not bow again.

So let the sky come crashing down,
I’ve already learned to stand my ground,
And if the world tries to pull me under,
I’ll come back louder, I’ll come back thunder.

songs.subject: dancing badly, swing
songs.lyrics:
I step out sharp in my two-left shoes,
Tryin’ to catch that rhythm and the swingin’ blues,
Band kicks off and I lose the beat,
My feet got plans they won’t repeat.
I spin too soon, I lean too late,
Still grinnin’ wide like it’s all first-rate,
Couples glide like they own the floor,
I’m just chaos in a vintage score.

But I’m dancin’ bad, and I’m feelin’ good,
Movin’ like I probably never should,
I trip, I twist, I laugh out loud,
Spinnin’ myself right into the crowd.
Ain’t got grace but I got this joy,
Shufflin’ round like a wind-up toy,
If this is wrong then I don’t care,
I’m swingin’ anyway in the Saturday air.

My hat flies off on a careless turn,
But I keep goin’ like I never learn,
Band hits a break and I miss the cue,
So I improvise like I’m s’posed to do.
Folks all watch with a puzzled grin,
But I bow like I meant that spin,
Floorboards creak to my clumsy beat,
And I make it work with my off-time feet.

Let ‘em dance clean, let ‘em dance tight,
I’m out here shakin’ up the night,
Every stumble just part of the show,
And I’m exactly where I wanna go.

songs.subject: town under reservoir, indie
songs.lyrics:
They raised the water where the church bells rang,
Where the old school yard still remembered your name,
Now the map just blurs into blue and gray,
And the road signs rust underwater decay.
I walk the ridge where the lookout stood,
Where your laughter once split the neighborhood,
But the valley’s gone, just a glassy sheet,
Hiding everything beneath.

And I swear I can still hear it below,
In the quiet undertow,
A town that’s breathing under the tide,
Where all our memories hide.
It’s sleeping deep where the current turns,
In the places that no light returns,
And I come back just to feel it again,
Like a ghost in the rain.

They said it was progress, said it was fate,
That the river needed a wider gate,
But I see rooftops when the wind is right,
Just beneath the shimmer at night.
Every ripple feels like a name I knew,
Every wave like a voice breaking through,
Calling out from a drowned-out past,
That refuses to pass.

If I whisper down to where you are,
Does it travel like a fallen star,
Through the water dark and wide and deep,
Where the vanished city sleeps?

songs.subject: studying at 2am, lo-fi
songs.lyrics:
2 a.m. glow on the cracked laptop screen,
Highlighter bleeding through the margin between,
Empty coffee cup, cold like my focus,
Thoughts drift off but I try to notice.
Pages stacking like unfinished dreams,
Time moving slower than it ever seems,
Rain on the window in a steady loop,
I replay mistakes in a tired group.
Headphones on but the silence creeps,
Even the bassline hums when the room won’t sleep,
I underline words I won’t remember,
Lost in the quiet of this long November.

And I’m studying alone at 2 a.m.,
Chasing daylight I can’t reach again,
Every problem feels like it knows my name,
Every answer looks the same.
Lo-fi heartbeat in a half-lit haze,
Trying to find my way through these days,
Just me and the page and the drifting sound,
In a world that’s slowing down.

songs.subject: unrequited love, mariachi
songs.lyrics:
I brought you roses wrapped in firelight,
Serenata under your window at night,
The trumpet cried what I could not say,
As you turned your face away.
The guitar trembled in my shaking hand,
Like it understood what I couldn’t stand,
That your heart was a closed-up door,
And I was knocking forevermore.

Ay, mi corazón, it beats for you alone,
Like a thunder in a hollow stone,
But you don’t hear the rhythm I bleed,
You don’t see how much I need.
I sing so loud the stars all shake,
Still you don’t feel the love I make,
Unrequited, I remain,
A love song lost in pouring rain.

The plaza echoes with my broken tune,
Beneath the watchful eye of a silver moon,
I pour my soul into every chord,
Still you walk like I’m ignored.
The brass explodes like a wounded sky,
Each note a question of “why, oh why,”
But you dance in another’s light,
While I dissolve into the night.

If I could trade this pain for dust,
I would scatter it, I must,
But even dust would spell your name,
Burning soft within the flame.

songs.subject: wordless couple, tender
songs.lyrics:
You hum in the kitchen, I know what it means,
It’s the soft kind of language between the routines,
A glance over coffee, a tilt of your head,
Says more than the words we never said.
We move like a rhythm that nobody hears,
Built out of silence over the years,
Even the pauses feel like home,
When we’re not alone.

We don’t need words, we don’t need sound,
To know what the other is thinking about,
It’s in the space where the quiet lives,
In the way your hand just fits.
Every look is a sentence I read,
Every breath is all I need,
We speak in the language of simply being,
You and me, understanding.

You turn the light down before I ask,
I already know what the moment asks,
We share the air like it’s second skin,
Letting the stillness in.
A thousand thoughts go unspoken here,
But nothing between us disappears,
We just exist in the same soft frame,
No need to name.

If the world went quiet and lost its voice,
We’d still know how to make the choice,
To find each other without a trace,
Just the shape of space.
songs.lyrics_en:
You hum in the kitchen, I know what it means,
It’s the soft kind of language between the routines,
A glance over coffee, a tilt of your head,
Says more than the words we never said.
We move like a rhythm that nobody hears,
Built out of silence over the years,
Even the pauses feel like home,
When we’re not alone.

We don’t need words, we don’t need sound,
To know what the other is thinking about,
It’s in the space where the quiet lives,
In the way your hand just fits.
Every look is a sentence I read,
Every breath is all I need,
We speak in the language of simply being,
You and me, understanding.

You turn the light down before I ask,
I already know what the moment asks,
We share the air like it’s second skin,
Letting the stillness in.
A thousand thoughts go unspoken here,
But nothing between us disappears,
We just exist in the same soft frame,
No need to name.

If the world went quiet and lost its voice,
We’d still know how to make the choice,
To find each other without a trace,
Just the shape of space.

songs.subject: factory closing, blues
songs.lyrics:
The whistle don’t blow like it used to do,
Just a hollow wind through the smokestack blue,
Lockers still rattlin’ in the empty hall,
But nobody answers the breakroom call.
Grease on the floor where the good times stood,
Machines gone cold in the ironwood,
Foreman reads it off a folded page,
“Shut it down for the modern age.”

And it’s goodbye, goodbye to the factory line,
Where we traded our hours for borrowed time,
Steel don’t cry but we sure do,
When the paycheck fades from view.
We built this town with a worn-out hand,
Now it’s sinking in the shifting sand,
One by one as the doors unwind,
It’s the end of the assembly line.

Old man Jenkins stares at the gate,
Says he knew this was comin’, just a matter of fate,
Lunch pails rustin’ in a busted rack,
No more shift to bring ’em back.
The clock still ticks but it don’t mean a thing,
No more punch cards, no more ring,
Just echoes driftin’ through the grime,
Of another slower crime.

If the furnace cools and the lights go black,
Ain’t no easy way to bring it back,
But we’ll remember in the dust and the haze,
The sound of our working days.

songs.subject: last night festival, house
songs.lyrics:
Neon sky fading into gold and flame,
Bass still shaking every voice, every name,
Shoes in the dust where the hours ran free,
We were everything we were meant to be.
Hands in the air like we’re holding the stars,
Lost in the echo of synth guitars,
Last set playing like it can’t be real,
But we feel what we feel what we feel.

This is the last night, don’t let it fade,
We’re dancing in the light that the memories made,
Heartbeat syncing with the four-on-floor,
We don’t want it to end anymore.
Raise it higher, let the moment ignite,
We are infinite in the flashing light,
One more drop and we disappear,
Together right here.

Confetti falling like a summer rain,
Every stranger knows your face by name,
We sing along with the fading sun,
Like the night has only just begun.
Speakers crying out their final hymn,
And we’re all glowing at the edge of the rim,
Of a moment that we can’t define,
But it’s yours and mine.

When the silence comes and the lights go down,
We’ll still hear that electric sound,
In every step when we leave this place,
It’ll echo in space.

songs.subject: procrastination diss, rap
songs.lyrics:
Yo, I’m beefin’ with myself, that lazy internal CEO,
Sleepin’ on my dreams like they rent-free in escrow,
“Start tomorrow,” yeah, that’s my favorite lie,
Got more “later” plans than stars in the sky.
I ghost my goals like they texted too soon,
Then blame Mercury, the clock, and the moon,
My focus got commitment issues, it flees,
I’m the only one sabotaging me with ease.
I sit down to work, then I “quickly” redecorate,
Watch videos on productivity I’ll never emulate,
My to-do list is a myth I keep extendin’,
Plot twist: I’m the villain I keep defending.

Pro-cras-ti-nation, you my worst opp,
Got me saying “five minutes” till the clock stop,
We been locked in a battle, no clear solution,
I keep losing to my own confusion.
Yeah, I roast myself but I never improve,
Diss track on me but I still don’t move,
One day I’ll win, but not today—
I’ll start this song… okay, okay, okay.

I’ll start this song… okay, okay, okay.

songs.subject: emigrating across sea, celtic
songs.lyrics:
The harbor sings in a mourning tide,
Where the cliffs fall steep and the gulls still cry,
I tied my name to a weathered rope,
Left behind the fields I know.
Mother stood where the lamplight fades,
With salt on her cheeks like the falling rain,
And father’s silence cut like stone,
As I turned toward the rolling foam.

Oh carry me over the darkened sea,
Where the winds don’t know my memory,
From the land of green to the unknown shore,
I am not who I was before.
Let the waves write what I cannot say,
As I drift from the world I leave today,
And the shore I love fades out of sight,
Into the endless night.

The ship creaks low like a broken hymn,
And the stars feel farther the deeper I swim,
Through oceans wide where the old dreams go,
And nothing familiar shows.
I hold your voice in the hollow air,
Like a thread I follow through the prayer,
Of tides that pull and tides that part,
And the ache that lives in the heart.

If I ever return to the morning green,
Will I still be the one you’ve seen,
Or just a ghost in a salt-stained coat,
On a drifting hope?

songs.subject: texting you shouldnt, bedroom-pop
songs.lyrics:
Blue glow on my ceiling at 1:03,
Thumbs hovering like it’s gravity,
Your name pops up like a bad idea,
And I already know why I’m here.
Typing “hey” then deleting it slow,
Like I’m trying to stop what I already know,
Soft little spiral in a quiet room,
It always leads back to you.

I shouldn’t text you, but I do,
Like I don’t know what it puts me through,
One little ping and I fall right in,
Over and over again.
It’s a late-night habit I can’t control,
You’re a cracked screen I still hold close,
I say I’m done but I never leave,
Just you and the ghost of me.

Seen on read but I still reply,
Like I’m chasing some softer goodbye,
Every message is a little mistake,
That I always choose to make.
The room is quiet but my phone is loud,
You’re a memory I keep digging out,
And I swear I’ll stop this time for real,
But I never will.

If I turned the phone face down and slept,
Would I finally forget the texts I sent,
Or would your name still light the dark,
Inside my heart?

songs.subject: faith hard times, gospel
songs.lyrics:
I walked through valleys where the light got thin,
Where every step felt like a reckoning,
Storms in my chest and dust in my name,
Still I whispered through the pain.
They said I’d break, said I’d fold in two,
But I learned what a soul can do,
When the ground gives way and the night gets long,
I find my song.

I won’t bow, I won’t bend,
Through the fire I’ll rise again,
Keep my faith when the world feels cold,
There’s a fire in my soul.
Hallelujah in the darkest hour,
I still believe in a higher power,
No chain can hold what grace has freed,
It’s still carrying me.

Every tear I cried was a seed in the ground,
Now I hear a different kind of sound,
Like angels walking through broken skies,
Singing truth I recognize.
And I’ve been down where the silence screams,
But I still hold onto brighter dreams,
‘Cause even when I lose my way,
I’m never far from grace.

So let the thunder roll, let the waters rise,
I’ve seen hope in a thousand tries,
And I’ll stand my ground till the end of days,
In a holy blaze.

songs.subject: city on sleeping giant, prog-rock
songs.lyrics:
Beneath the fog where iron rivers bend,
A city sleeps where ancient roots descend,
Steel spires grow from bone and basalt stone,
Built on a giant who dreams alone.
His breath is coal beneath the streets,
His heartbeat shakes the subways deep,
We carve our lives in his drifting skin,
Never knowing where he ends and we begin.

Oh, rise not yet, you sleeping titan under land,
Hold your thunder in your hand,
We dance on echoes of your flame,
Calling shadows by your name.
If you wake, will we remain,
Or drown inside your ancient reign?
Oh, cradle us in fractured time,
Great stone heart beneath the skyline.

The towers lean like questions in the rain,
Whispering secrets through electric veins,
Every window is an eye that cannot see,
Every road runs through a memory.
Trains descend like prayers underground,
Where the giant turns without a sound,
And architects in glass and light
Build castles on his dreaming night.

Oh, rise not yet, you sleeping titan under land,
Hold your thunder in your hand,
We dance on echoes of your flame,
Calling shadows by your name.
If you wake, will we remain,
Or dissolve into your ancient reign?
Oh, cradle us in fractured time,
Great stone heart beneath the skyline.

And in the deepest tunnel far below,
A low vibration starts to grow,
The giant stirs, the mortar sings,
Steel remembers it has wings.
We hear the pulse in every wire,
A slow cathedral of buried fire,
And all our futures crack and bend
Where his dreaming thoughts descend.

Oh, rise not yet, you sleeping titan under land,
Hold your thunder in your hand,
We are whispers in your vein,
Fragile sparks against your reign.
If you wake, we’ll understand
We were never more than sand.
Oh, cradle us in fractured time,
Great stone heart beneath the skyline.

songs.subject: a soul song about a night-shift nurse
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights shimmer on the hospital glass
Midnight rolls in and the hours crawl past
Coffee getting colder by the nurses' station
She keeps moving with a quiet dedication
A frightened child with tears in their eyes
An old man staring at the ceiling lights
She holds a hand when the room feels lonely
Giving strength when hope is hanging slowly

She's the heartbeat of the night shift
Walking through the shadows with a light so bright
When the world is sleeping, she's still giving
Pouring out her soul till the morning sky
She carries every worry, every prayer
With tired feet and kindness everywhere
She's the heartbeat of the night shift
Keeping love alive through the night

The monitors sing their electric songs
She knows which rooms need her before too long
Every chart, every call, every moment
A thousand little battles and she's in them
She misses birthdays, misses Friday nights
Trading comfort for fluorescent lights
But every life she touches leaves a trace
Like a sunrise shining softly on her face

She's the heartbeat of the night shift
Walking through the shadows with a light so bright
When the world is sleeping, she's still giving
Pouring out her soul till the morning sky
She carries every worry, every prayer
With tired feet and kindness everywhere
She's the heartbeat of the night shift
Keeping love alive through the night

And when the dawn comes breaking through the blinds
She feels the weight of every life and time
Still she smiles, knowing she made it through
Another night of doing what she was born to do

She's the heartbeat of the night shift
Walking through the shadows with a light so bright
When the world is sleeping, she's still giving
Pouring out her soul till the morning sky
She carries every worry, every prayer
With tired feet and kindness everywhere
She's the heartbeat of the night shift
Keeping love alive through the night

The morning paints the hallways gold and blue
She heads home as a brand-new day breaks through
And though nobody sees all she's been giving
The world keeps turning because of how she's living

songs.subject: a tango song about the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Midnight drapes the harbor in velvet blue and gray,
A bandoneón is sighing where the silent shadows sway.
I trace your name on frost upon the window of the night,
While emerald ribbons gather in a river made of light.

The northern sky is dancing with a secret in its eyes,
Turning cold horizons into burning paradise.
Every step we take together leaves a spark upon the snow,
As the stars lean down to listen to the music far below.

Aurora, spin me slowly through your silver-green embrace,
Paint your trembling colors on the darkness of this place.
Like a tango of the heavens, you appear and drift away,
Yet your fire lingers with me till the breaking of the day.

The wind becomes an orchestra across the frozen land,
And every glowing curtain moves as if by your command.
You pull me close and suddenly the world begins to turn,
In the lantern-lit silence where the northern wonders burn.

If dawn should steal the colors and the sky return to stone,
I will keep your shining footsteps in the chambers of my soul.
For one enchanted moment, all the distance disappeared,
And the night itself was singing everything we longed to hear.

Aurora, spin me slowly through your silver-green embrace,
Paint your trembling colors on the darkness of this place.
Like a tango of the heavens, you appear and drift away,
Yet your fire lingers with me till the breaking of the day.

Aurora, spin me slowly through your silver-green embrace,
Paint your trembling colors on the darkness of this place.
Like a tango of the heavens, you appear and drift away,
Yet your fire lingers with me till the breaking of the day.

songs.subject: a flamenco song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Dust on my boots and a scar in the rain,
Years on the road carrying sorrow and flame,
The drums have gone silent, the banners are torn,
I follow the moon to the house where I was born.
The river remembers the sound of my name,
The olive trees whisper though nothing's the same,
I left as a young man with fire in my chest,
I return with the shadows that never will rest.

Ay, open the door, let the candlelight shine,
I've crossed through the darkness to stand by your side,
The war took its payment in blood and in time,
But my heart found the path and brought me home alive.
Ay, hold me close, let the old songs begin,
The wind of the mountains is calling again,
I carried the thunder, I carry the pain,
But tonight I am home in your arms once again.

The fields wear the gold of a late summer sun,
Children are laughing where battles were won,
I kneel on the threshold and touch the cold stone,
For a soldier can wander yet dream of one home.
I hear my mother's voice drift through the air,
I see every promise still waiting there,
The nights were so long with the cannons' refrain,
Now the guitars are singing and washing the stain.

I buried my fear where the wild roses grow,
I buried the faces I could not let go,
The stars over heaven have witnessed it all,
The rise of the brave and the proud when they fall.
And though I still tremble when midnight bells ring,
I know there is mercy in each dawn it brings,
The road may have changed me, the years may have flown,
But love kept a lantern to guide me back home.

Ay, open the door, let the candlelight shine,
I've crossed through the darkness to stand by your side,
The war took its payment in blood and in time,
But my heart found the path and brought me home alive.
Ay, hold me close, let the old songs begin,
The wind of the mountains is calling again,
I carried the thunder, I carry the pain,
But tonight I am home in your arms once again.

But my heart found the path and brought me home alive.
Ay, hold me close, let the old songs begin,
The wind of the mountains is calling again,
I carried the thunder, I carry the pain,
But tonight I am home in your arms once again.

songs.subject: a klezmer song about a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
Down Rampart Street the trumpet cried,
A silver tear in the summer tide,
The tuba rolled like distant thunder,
While mourners marched and skies grew under.
A clarinet bent every note,
Like a prayer caught in a riverboat,
And old men swayed in black parade,
Through cypress shade where memories stayed.

Oy, carry him slow through the heat and the haze,
Then lift up your feet when the sorrow gives way,
From tears to dancing, from dusk into dawn,
The soul keeps traveling after it's gone.
Hey! Let the brass and the fiddle agree,
Life is a river that runs to the sea,
Oy, sing hallelujah, sing l'chaim today,
The dead have departed, the living will play.

A widow hummed a Yiddish tune,
Soft as the glow of a harvest moon,
The drummer tapped a second line,
Keeping faithful, keeping time.
The rabbi smiled through reddened eyes,
At pelicans crossing the amber skies,
And every step along the way
Turned grief to gold in the light of day.

Oy, carry him slow through the heat and the haze,
Then lift up your feet when the sorrow gives way,
From tears to dancing, from dusk into dawn,
The soul keeps traveling after it's gone.
Hey! Let the brass and the fiddle agree,
Life is a river that runs to the sea,
Oy, sing hallelujah, sing l'chaim today,
The dead have departed, the living will play.

The horn called out, the fiddle replied,
Two distant worlds walking side by side,
From the old shtetl roads to the Crescent shore,
Different doors, but the same great door.
Ashes and laughter, blessing and rain,
What leaves this world comes back again,
In every heartbeat, every refrain,
We lose, we love, we remember the name.

Now the band is bright and the street is alive,
Children spin and neighbors jive,
The trumpet shines where tears once fell,
Ringing joy through the farewell bell.
And somewhere beyond the river's bend,
A weary traveler has found a friend,
While here below we raise a song,
And carry the melody along.

Oy, carry him slow through the heat and the haze,
Then lift up your feet when the sorrow gives way,
From tears to dancing, from dusk into dawn,
The soul keeps traveling after it's gone.
Hey! Let the brass and the fiddle agree,
Life is a river that runs to the sea,
Oy, sing hallelujah, sing l'chaim today,
The dead have departed, the living will play.

songs.subject: a lo-fi hip hop song about a factory closing down
songs.lyrics:
Night shift coffee gone cold by the gate,
Rain on the windows, clock running late,
Forklifts sleeping where the assembly lines hum,
A note on the bulletin board says the end has come.
Steel-toe memories scattered on the floor,
Names carved in lockers that won't open anymore,
The whistle stays silent in the pale sunrise,
Dust hangs heavy where the future used to rise.

The factory lights fade into the blue,
Smoke stacks drawing ghosts across the view,
We built our days one bolt at a time,
Now the machines rest with the rhythm and rhyme,
And the town slows down with the evening train,
Holding on to echoes through the quiet rain.

Parking lot empty like a half-finished song,
Everybody wondering where they belong,
Paychecks turned to papers folded in a hand,
Dreams packed carefully in a cardboard van.
Old radio playing from a security room,
Soft static drifting through the gathering gloom,
The conveyor belts stop, but the years remain,
Pressed into the concrete like a permanent stain.

The factory lights fade into the blue,
Smoke stacks drawing ghosts across the view,
We built our days one bolt at a time,
Now the machines rest with the rhythm and rhyme,
And the town slows down with the evening train,
Holding on to echoes through the quiet rain.

Maybe tomorrow brings a different road,
Maybe new hands will carry the load,
But tonight the silence fills every hall,
Like the last loose screw after it falls.

The factory lights fade into the blue,
Smoke stacks drawing ghosts across the view,
We built our days one bolt at a time,
Now the machines rest with the rhythm and rhyme,
And the town slows down with the evening train,
Holding on to echoes through the quiet rain.

songs.subject: a soul song about watching your father grow old
songs.lyrics:
Your hands used to lift the whole world high
Carried every burden without asking why
Now I watch you searching for a name or a place
Time has drawn its story in the lines upon your face

The porch light glows on another quiet night
You move a little slower in its amber light
I remember running while you called me home
Now I walk beside you so you won't walk alone

And it breaks my heart, but it fills me with grace
To see the years settling softly in your face
The man who seemed taller than the sky above
Is teaching me one more time the meaning of love
Though the seasons take their turn and days grow cold
I'm learning how to love my father growing old

The stories come in pieces, but I know them all
The victories, the struggles, every rise and fall
And when your voice grows quiet, I still hear the sound
Of the strength you gave our family year after year around

If I could borrow time, I'd hold it in my hands
Keep every laugh and lesson exactly as they stand
But life keeps moving onward like a river to the sea
So I'll treasure every moment that remains for you and me

And it breaks my heart, but it fills me with grace
To see the years settling softly in your face
The man who seemed taller than the sky above
Is teaching me one more time the meaning of love
Though the seasons take their turn and days grow cold
I'm learning how to love my father growing old

And when the evening shadows stretch across the floor
I'll sit beside you gladly like you've done before
Through every changing chapter, one truth I'll always hold
My hero is still here, even growing old

songs.subject: a jazz song about the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The clock keeps time with a lazy sway,
A muted trumpet at the end of day,
Your coffee cools where your hand had been,
And the room wears the shape of what might have been.
The words we threw are gone from sight,
Just smoke rings fading in the light,
The window hums with the passing rain,
And silence learns how to speak again.

In the quiet after the fight,
When the shadows soften the night,
Every heartbeat asks your name,
And nothing here feels quite the same.
No more sparks, no more fire,
Just the echo of desire,
In the quiet after the fight,
I still reach for you tonight.

The record skips on a worn-out groove,
Like two stubborn souls afraid to move,
Your laughter lingers in the hall,
A ghost of summer on the wall.
I trace the lines on an empty glass,
Watching restless reflections pass,
The city sighs beyond the door,
But I don't know what I'm waiting for.

In the quiet after the fight,
When the shadows soften the night,
Every heartbeat asks your name,
And nothing here feels quite the same.
No more sparks, no more fire,
Just the echo of desire,
In the quiet after the fight,
I still reach for you tonight.

Maybe tomorrow we'll find a tune,
Some tender melody beneath the moon,
Maybe these broken notes will bend,
And lead us back where we began again.
The night is deep, the band plays low,
There's so much left we never let show,
And though the silence fills the air,
I swear I feel you somewhere there.

In the quiet after the fight,
When the shadows soften the night,
Every heartbeat asks your name,
And nothing here feels quite the same.
No more sparks, no more fire,
Just the echo of desire,
In the quiet after the fight,
I still reach for you tonight.

The last blue note hangs soft and thin,
Between goodbye and letting in,
And as the dawn begins to rise,
I find forgiveness in your eyes.

songs.subject: a mariachi song about a New Orleans funeral
songs.lyrics:
The river rolls slow by the old parade line,
Black coats shining in the Louisiana sun,
A trumpet cries out where the cypress trees sway,
And the drums keep time for a life well done.
Through the French Quarter streets we carry his name,
Past balconies dressed in flowers and rain,
The saints and the sinners all walk side by side,
Singing farewell as we dance through the pain.

Ay, toca la guitarra, let the heavens hear,
Raise your voices high, no room for fear,
From sorrow to joy, we follow the tune,
Beneath the bright stars and the silver moon.
Though tears fill our eyes, we will not stand still,
Love marches onward beyond every hill,
In New Orleans we send him home this way,
With a song in our hearts and a band that plays.

The brass rings golden in the afternoon light,
The snare drum rattles like a distant train,
Mothers are praying, children are smiling,
Holding memories tighter than the rain.
The river remembers every soul that passed,
Every laugh and promise carried downstream,
So we lift our hats and we lift our voices,
And send him away on a beautiful dream.

Ay, toca la guitarra, let the heavens hear,
Raise your voices high, no room for fear,
From sorrow to joy, we follow the tune,
Beneath the bright stars and the silver moon.
Though tears fill our eyes, we will not stand still,
Love marches onward beyond every hill,
In New Orleans we send him home this way,
With a song in our hearts and a band that plays.

When the last note rises over the water,
And the evening settles soft and blue,
We will remember the stories he gave us,
Every road he walked, every dream he knew.
No goodbye can silence the echo,
No grave can hold a melody,
His spirit is dancing with the angels,
Forever wild and forever free.

Ay, toca la guitarra, let the heavens hear,
Raise your voices high, no room for fear,
From sorrow to joy, we follow the tune,
Beneath the bright stars and the silver moon.
Though tears fill our eyes, we will not stand still,
Love marches onward beyond every hill,
In New Orleans we send him home this way,
With a song in our hearts and a band that plays.

songs.subject: a R&B song about the northern lights
songs.lyrics:
Midnight drapes across the frozen shore,
Silver stars are knocking at my door,
Then the sky begins to move for you,
Painting emerald dreams in every hue.
Your hand in mine, the world fades away,
Colors dancing where the shadows stay,
Every heartbeat glows against the night,
Wrapped inside a river made of light.

Northern lights, falling through the dark,
Leaving little constellations on my heart,
Green and gold, swaying soft and slow,
Like a secret only heaven seems to know.
Northern lights, shining in your eyes,
Turning every ordinary moment into skies,
Hold me close, don't let this feeling go,
We're glowing with the northern lights tonight.

Silent snow beneath our wandering feet,
Cold air, but your love feels like heat,
Every ribbon curling overhead,
Tracing all the words we never said.
Time stands still beneath the crystal glow,
Watching all the colors overflow,
In the distance, night and wonder blend,
Like a melody that never wants to end.

Northern lights, falling through the dark,
Leaving little constellations on my heart,
Green and gold, swaying soft and slow,
Like a secret only heaven seems to know.
Northern lights, shining in your eyes,
Turning every ordinary moment into skies,
Hold me close, don't let this feeling go,
We're glowing with the northern lights tonight.

If the morning comes and steals the view,
I'll still see those colors inside you,
Every wave of light across the sky,
Lives forever in this love tonight.

Northern lights, falling through the dark,
Leaving little constellations on my heart,
Green and gold, swaying soft and slow,
Like a secret only heaven seems to know.
Northern lights, shining in your eyes,
Turning every ordinary moment into skies,
Hold me close, don't let this feeling go,
We're glowing with the northern lights tonight.

songs.subject: a gospel song about a summer fling at the beach
songs.lyrics:
Golden sunlight dancing on the rolling tide,
I met you where the sea and sky were open wide,
Barefoot in the sand, laughter on the breeze,
A summer spark was shining through the swaying trees.

We walked along the shoreline as the day grew long,
The waves became a rhythm and the gulls a song,
Though our hearts were drifting like the boats at sea,
I felt the grace of heaven in your company.

Hallelujah, thank You for the days we shared,
For the warmth of summer and the love that cared,
Like a passing sunset, beautiful and bright,
You were a blessing in my life for a season's time.

We watched the colors fading as the evening came,
And prayed beneath the starlight, calling on Your name,
Though the tide was turning and we'd soon move on,
We trusted You would guide us when the summer's gone.

Every wave returns to where it's meant to be,
Every road is held within Your mystery,
If our paths should wander to another shore,
We'll carry all the joy You gave forevermore.

Hallelujah, thank You for the days we shared,
For the warmth of summer and the love that cared,
Like a passing sunset, beautiful and bright,
You were a blessing in my life for a season's time.

Hallelujah, thank You for Your faithful hand,
For the moments written gently in the sand,
Though the season ended, still Your love remains,
And Your light keeps shining through the joy and change.

songs.subject: a R&B song about two astronauts drifting apart
songs.lyrics:
We floated off the pad, heartbeats in the comms
Two silhouettes in silence, where the gravity was gone
You said “hold my signal,” I said “don’t fade away”
But stars don’t make promises, they just burn and stray
We learned to walk on nothing, we learned to breathe in dark
Counting down our distance in the echo of the spark
Your voice got a little softer every orbit I could feel
Like the space between us started replacing what was real

Now we’re drifting, drifting out past the line
Two lost satellites running out of time
I reach for you but you’re a million miles gone
In this endless night, we keep moving on
We’re drifting, drifting where the signals break
Love on a frequency we couldn’t quite remake
If I call your name, does it echo where you are?
Or are you just another fading star

We mapped our broken orbit like a language we once knew
But every path we charted only led me far from you
The mission kept on spinning, but the mission wasn’t clear
Was it finding new horizons or just learning how to disappear
I saw you through the glass like a memory in flame
Your face a distant planet that I couldn’t ever name
And every time I tried to turn this craft back toward your light
The universe would push me further into colder night

Now we’re drifting, drifting out past the line
Two lost satellites running out of time
I reach for you but you’re a million miles gone
In this endless night, we keep moving on
We’re drifting, drifting where the signals break
Love on a frequency we couldn’t quite remake
If I call your name, does it echo where you are?
Or are you just another fading star

If time bends back around, would we still collide?
Or are we just two comets passing in the tide?
I’m still sending you the light I couldn’t say out loud
But space keeps swallowing every vow
Maybe love was never meant to leave the atmosphere
Maybe we were just too human to stay here
So I let you drift, I let you go unseen
Two astronauts dissolving in between

Now we’re drifting, drifting out past the line
Two lost satellites running out of time
I reach for you but you’re a million miles gone
In this endless night, we keep moving on
We’re drifting, drifting where the signals break
Love on a frequency we couldn’t quite remake
If I call your name, does it echo where you are?
Or are you just another fading star

Now we’re drifting, drifting out past the line
Two lost satellites running out of time
I reach for you but you’re a million miles gone
In this endless night, we keep moving on
We’re drifting, drifting where the signals break
Love on a frequency we couldn’t quite remake
If I call your name, does it echo where you are?
Or are you just another fading star

If time bends back around, would we still collide?
Or are we just two comets passing in the tide?
I’m still sending you the light I couldn’t say out loud
But space keeps swallowing every vow
Maybe love was never meant to leave the atmosphere
Maybe we were just too human to stay here
So I let you drift, I let you go unseen
Two astronauts dissolving in between

Now we’re drifting, drifting out past the line
Two lost satellites running out of time
I reach for you but you’re a million miles gone
In this endless night, we keep moving on
We’re drifting, drifting where the signals break
Love on a frequency we couldn’t quite remake
If I call your name, does it echo where you are?
Or are you just another fading star

songs.subject: a gospel song about the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
I was walking through the shadow of a fading light
With my hands held empty, trying to do what’s right
Every door I knocked on turned to silent stone
And I felt so far away from ever making it home
I could hear the promise like a distant drum
Saying hold on child, your breakin’ day will come
But the night kept whispering, “you’ve gone too far”
Like hope was just a memory of who you are

It was the night I almost made it, almost saw the dawn
Almost laid my burdens down and found where I belong
But mercy met me in the middle of my broken fight
And carried me through the dark when I ran out of light
Oh, I was so close to giving up the fight
But grace said “not tonight”

I remember every step like a heavy chain
Every prayer I whispered through the pouring rain
I was one breath away from letting go
When I felt a still small voice say “don’t you know”
That every tear was counted, every wound was seen
Even in the wreckage I was still redeemed
So I turned my face toward a hope I couldn’t see
And something stronger than the night broke over me

It was the night I almost made it, almost saw the dawn
Almost laid my burdens down and found where I belong
But mercy met me in the middle of my broken fight
And carried me through the dark when I ran out of light
Oh, I was so close to giving up the fight
But grace said “not tonight”

And if I ever forget how far You brought me through
I’ll remember the darkness where You broke in true
When I was standing on the edge of giving in
You were the hand that pulled me back again

It was the night I almost made it, almost saw the dawn
Almost laid my burdens down and found where I belong
But mercy met me in the middle of my broken fight
And carried me through the dark when I ran out of light
Oh, I was so close to giving up the fight
But grace said “not tonight”

songs.subject: a swing song about calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
The phone booth light was buzzing like a jitterbug tune,
I stood there in the evening, under a tired old moon.
My thumb on the dial like a memory on fire,
Every ring pulled me closer, every silence went higher.
I hadn’t heard your laughter since the days of my shame,
Just a ghost in my pocket and me calling your name.

Hello, Mama, it’s me on the line,
After all of this distance, I hope you don’t mind.
I’ve been walking through years I don’t know how to explain,
But I’m calling you now through the joy and the pain.
If you’re still there on the other side of the wire,
Say my name like you used to, and light up the fire.

There were letters I wrote but I never could send,
And a pride that was louder than the voice of a friend.
I built all these fences I thought I could defend,
Now I’m tearing them down just to hear you again.
The city keeps swinging, but I stand still,
With a heart full of questions I’m scared you might fill.

Hello, Mama, it’s me on the line,
After all of this distance, I hope you don’t mind.
I’ve been walking through years I don’t know how to explain,
But I’m calling you now through the joy and the pain.
If you’re still there on the other side of the wire,
Say my name like you used to, and light up the fire.

If the years made a stranger out of flesh and bone,
Let the sound of my voice bring me back home.
I don’t need perfection, just a chance to begin,
To fold up the silence I was living in.
So breathe in the moment, let the past unwind,
I’m reaching through the distance, hope I’m not too late to find—

Hello, Mama, it’s me on the line,
After all of this distance, I hope you don’t mind.
I’ve been walking through years I don’t know how to explain,
But I’m calling you now through the joy and the pain.
If you’re still there on the other side of the wire,
Say my name like you used to, and light up the fire.

songs.subject: a soul song about the night you almost made it
songs.lyrics:
I still hear the hum of that city on fire
Neon bleeding through the wires of my tired desire
You said “keep going, don’t look back now”
But the night got heavy and I don’t know how
We were running on hope and a half-lit dream
Barely breathing, chasing something unseen
I had your voice in the pocket of my coat
Like a lifeline I was scared to hope

We almost made it, I swear we were right there
Touching the edge of a life we could wear
But the dawn came early, stole what we’d planned
And left me with dust in my hands
We almost made it, don’t say it’s gone
It still burns in me when the night comes on
Like a song that fades but won’t let me be
That night you almost made it with me

There was laughter in the back of a borrowed car
Fading like a promise that didn’t go far
Streetlights folding into the rain
Every mile felt like breaking a chain
You were closer than I’ve ever known
Still I felt like I was walking alone
We reached for forever with shaking hands
But forever don’t always do what it plans

We almost made it, I swear we were right there
Touching the edge of a life we could wear
But the dawn came early, stole what we’d planned
And left me with dust in my hands
We almost made it, don’t say it’s gone
It still burns in me when the night comes on
Like a song that fades but won’t let me be
That night you almost made it with me

If I close my eyes I can still see the line
Where your shadow met up perfectly with mine
And I wonder if the world just blinked too fast
Or if we were never meant to make it last

We almost made it, I swear we were right there
Touching the edge of a life we could wear
But the dawn came early, stole what we’d planned
And left me with dust in my hands
We almost made it, but I still believe
Somewhere that night didn’t let us leave
It lives in the dark, it breathes in me
That night you almost made it with me

songs.subject: a swing song about the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The lights are flickerin’ like fireflies in a jar
We’re dancing in the dust where the memories are
Confetti on the pavement, glitter in your hair
Last night of the festival, but we don’t care
The band plays tired but they swing it like a dream
Trumpet notes are spillin’ like a late-night stream
We laugh a little louder than we ought to do
Because tomorrow’s empty, and tonight is true

Oh, swing me one more time ‘round the faded fairground light
Hold me like the music’s gonna end tonight
We’re spinning through the goodbye, chasing what we can’t keep
On the last night of the festival, before the city sleeps
Oh, swing me one more time, don’t let the moment fade
We’re golden in the chaos that the night parade made
If this is just a memory, let it burn so bright
On the last night of the festival, we’re alive tonight

The carousel is slowing but it hums along
Like it doesn’t want to finish its old, worn song
We carve our names in echoes only night can read
Between the empty stalls and the trampled seed
A vendor packs the laughter into cardboard crates
While somewhere someone’s dancing with the hands of fate
We steal a final waltz from a borrowed tune
Underneath a paper sky and a crooked moon

Oh, swing me one more time ‘round the faded fairground light
Hold me like the music’s gonna end tonight
We’re spinning through the goodbye, chasing what we can’t keep
On the last night of the festival, before the city sleeps
Oh, swing me one more time, don’t let the moment fade
We’re golden in the chaos that the night parade made
If this is just a memory, let it burn so bright
On the last night of the festival, we’re alive tonight

When the dawn slips in like a quiet thief
And the band puts down its final grief
We’ll be just a echo in the empty air
But for now we’re everywhere, everywhere
So hold the note, don’t let it fall
Let the night pretend it never ends at all

Oh, swing me one more time ‘round the faded fairground light
Hold me like the music’s gonna end tonight
We’re spinning through the goodbye, chasing what we can’t keep
On the last night of the festival, before the city sleeps
Oh, swing me one more time, don’t let the moment fade
We’re golden in the chaos that the night parade made
If this is just a memory, let it burn so bright
On the last night of the festival, we’re alive tonight

songs.subject: a ska song about an underdog team's final game
songs.lyrics:
Woke up with the bleachers still empty in my head
Tape on our sneakers and a knot of words unsaid
Coach says “boys, it’s do or die, no safety net today”
But we’ve been counted out so long, it’s just the way they say
We ride that bus like it’s a rocket to the end
Every dent in the metal feels like an old friend
They’ve got the trophies lined up, polished bright and tall
But we’ve got something different burning through it all

Hey, hey, we’re the last ones standing in the rain
Underdogs dancing through the pain
We might be small but we’re loud when we collide
Watch us turn the whole game upside
Hey, hey, hear the crowd start to sway
Nothing left to lose today
We’re not supposed to make it, but we came anyway
In this final game

First whistle hits like thunder rolling through the stands
Their eyes all say we’re just a footnote in their plans
But every pass we throw is lightning in disguise
And every step we take is fire in our eyes
They score first, yeah they always do it clean
Like they wrote the story long before the scene
But we’re scribbling over every line they planned
With muddy shoes and grit we barely understand

Hey, hey, we’re the last ones standing in the rain
Underdogs dancing through the pain
We might be small but we’re loud when we collide
Watch us turn the whole game upside
Hey, hey, hear the crowd start to sway
Nothing left to lose today
We’re not supposed to make it, but we came anyway
In this final game

And in the noise I hear my heartbeat sync with the skank
Every loss we ever had just fueling the tank
One more pass, one more run, one more breath we steal
And suddenly the impossible starts to feel real
The clock is screaming down like it’s got something to prove
But we’re moving like a rhythm they can’t refuse
One last play, one last chance, everything on the line
And the whole damn stadium starts to come alive

Hey, hey, we’re the last ones standing in the rain
Underdogs dancing through the pain
We might be small but we’re loud when we collide
Watch us turn the whole game upside
Hey, hey, hear the crowd start to sway
Nothing left to lose today
We’re not supposed to make it, but we came anyway
In this final game

songs.subject: a hip hop song about teaching your daughter to drive
songs.lyrics:
Keys in my hand, I said “let’s take it slow”
You gripping the wheel like you ready to go
First time on the road, heart beating in sync
I see you trying hard not to overthink
Blinkers on, mirror check, breath held tight
Sunset on the dash turning day into night
You ask if you’re doing it right, I say “you’ll learn”
Every mistake is just another turn
I remember when I was where you stand
Now I’m in the passenger, trusting your plan
Little kid in the backseat asking for a song
Now you in the front tryna prove you belong

I’m teaching you to drive, but it’s more than the road
It’s knowing when to brake, when to carry the load
Keep your eyes ahead, don’t fear the unknown
I won’t always be here, but you’re never alone
Hands on the wheel, heart in the line
Every mile you grow, you’re one step in time
I’m right here beside you, steady and proud
Watch you find your way through the crowd

Round the block twice, now you easing the pace
Got that look of focus, calm in your face
Stalling at the light, but you restart clean
Learning every lesson in between
I don’t raise my voice, I just point and guide
You correcting yourself, building that drive inside
Potholes come quick, you adjust and steer
Confidence growing every time you shift gears
Used to hold your bike when you first learned balance
Now you navigating every new challenge
I’m quiet in the seat, just watching you grow
Every stop sign teaching you what you didn’t know

I’m teaching you to drive, but it’s more than the road
It’s knowing when to brake, when to carry the load
Keep your eyes ahead, don’t fear the unknown
I won’t always be here, but you’re never alone
Hands on the wheel, heart in the line
Every mile you grow, you’re one step in time
I’m right here beside you, steady and proud
Watch you find your way through the crowd

One day you’ll go alone on streets I don’t see
But you’ll still hear my voice in your memory
Every lesson now is a seed that I sow
So you can drive further than I’ll ever go
If you ever get lost, just breathe and recall
It’s not about speed, it’s about standing tall
I’ll be your calm in the rearview glass
Even when the moment has already passed

songs.subject: a synthwave song about the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
Neon bruised sky over a paper-thin crowd
Confetti still falling like it forgot how to stop
We’re counting down echoes in a glittering loud
While the Ferris wheel hums its electric drop
Your jacket smells like smoke and summer rain
We carved our names in the glow of a dying stage
Every heartbeat synced to a synth refrain
As the night rewinds on a flickering page

It’s the last night of the festival
Under ultraviolet skies
We’re chasing ghosts in the stereo
With static in our eyes
Hold me close in the overload
Let the moment crystallize
It’s the last night of the festival
And we don’t say goodbye

Broken wristbands and a melted ice drink
We’re dancing where the asphalt meets the stars
The bassline pulls us right to the brink
Like a signal sent from somewhere far
Laser lights cut through the fading air
Like memories glitching in a looped cassette
You laugh like nothing could ever care
That tomorrow hasn’t happened yet

It’s the last night of the festival
Under ultraviolet skies
We’re chasing ghosts in the stereo
With static in our eyes
Hold me close in the overload
Let the moment crystallize
It’s the last night of the festival
And we don’t say goodbye

When the speakers die and the crowds all fade
We’ll be silhouettes in the afterglow
Two lost signals in the cascade
Of a world we’ll never quite let go
So if this ends in a soft rewind
Let it loop us back where we began
Two hearts tangled in neon time
Running forever through shifting sand

It’s the last night of the festival
Under ultraviolet skies
We’re chasing ghosts in the stereo
With static in our eyes
Hold me close in the overload
Let the moment crystallize
It’s the last night of the festival
And we don’t say goodbye

So if this ends in a soft rewind
Let it loop us back where we began
Two hearts tangled in neon time
Running forever through shifting sand

It’s the last night of the festival
Under ultraviolet skies
We’re chasing ghosts in the stereo
With static in our eyes
Hold me close in the overload
Let the moment crystallize
It’s the last night of the festival
And we don’t say goodbye

songs.subject: a klezmer song about a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
In the park where the old men gather under chestnut shade,
He sets up a folding table where his little games are played.
A worn wooden chessboard, a cigarette glow,
“Step right up, friend, if you think that you know.”
He smiles like a secret, half saint, half scam,
Says “I play for the coins, but I play who I am.”

Oy, the kings fall dancing, the pawns run thin,
In the hustler’s garden where the games begin.
Tap your foot to the rhythm, let the tension rise,
Every move is a story, every bluff is a lie.
Oy, in the park where the river winds,
You don’t just lose— you leave pieces behind.

A tourist from Bremen, a student from town,
They sit down too confident, they don’t look around.
He offers them mercy with a crooked grin,
Then he takes all their bishops and drinks it all in.
The pigeons are judges, they nod from above,
As he teaches humility, wrapped up in love.

Oy, the kings fall dancing, the pawns run thin,
In the hustler’s garden where the games begin.
Tap your foot to the rhythm, let the tension rise,
Every move is a story, every bluff is a lie.
Oy, in the park where the river winds,
You don’t just lose— you leave pieces behind.

But late in the evening when the light turns gold,
He plays a blind beggar with fingers ice-cold.
No coins on the table, no laughter, no boast,
Just two quiet shadows like a half-finished toast.
And for once in his life he’s not sure of the score,
As the beggar just whispers “I’ve seen you before.”

So spin like a violin, sharp and unkind,
Every move is a memory you can’t leave behind.
The park keeps its secrets in rustling leaves,
Even hustlers are players of tricks they believe.

Oy, the kings fall dancing, the pawns run thin,
In the hustler’s garden where the games begin.
Tap your foot to the rhythm, let the tension rise,
Every move is a story, every bluff is a lie.
Oy, in the park where the river winds,
You don’t just lose— you leave pieces behind.

songs.subject: a afrobeats song about a diner that stays open all night
songs.lyrics:
City never sleep, neon on the chrome
Midnight on the road, but I don’t feel alone
Smell of fresh coffee when I push through the door
Same old diner humming like it’s asking for more
Cook flipping patties like a late-night groove
Radio playing something with a steady smooth move
Strangers in the booths telling stories out loud
All of us lost but we found here somehow

At the all-night diner, where the hours don’t close
Where the night keeps talking and everybody knows
We be dancing in the glow of the coffee machine
Living in a moment that’s a midnight dream
All-night diner, stay a little bit longer
Heartbeats syncing like a low bass drummer
When the world goes quiet, this place gets stronger
All-night diner, hold us a little longer

Rain on the window like a soft percussion
Every table got a late-night discussion
Truckers, lovers, dreamers on a break
Trying to find something real they can take
Waitress smiling like she seen it before
Broken hearts walking right back through the door
Jukebox spinning old-school affection
Every song a little late-night confession

At the all-night diner, where the hours don’t close
Where the night keeps talking and everybody knows
We be dancing in the glow of the coffee machine
Living in a moment that’s a midnight dream
All-night diner, stay a little bit longer
Heartbeats syncing like a low bass drummer
When the world goes quiet, this place gets stronger
All-night diner, hold us a little longer

When the city fades and the sky turns deep
We find a rhythm in the space we keep
No morning rush can break this spell
In this small warm light, we know ourselves

At the all-night diner, where the hours don’t close
Where the night keeps talking and everybody knows
We be dancing in the glow of the coffee machine
Living in a moment that’s a midnight dream
All-night diner, stay a little bit longer
Heartbeats syncing like a low bass drummer
When the world goes quiet, this place gets stronger
All-night diner, hold us a little longer

songs.subject: a shoegaze song about a widower learning to cook for one
songs.lyrics:
Steam on the window like a memory refusing to leave
Two cups in the sink but I only rinse one clean
The kettle hums a language I almost understand
Your absence sits quiet in the chair where you used to stand
I learn the weight of silence measured in teaspoons
How bread tastes different when it’s only for one spoon
Radio static like waves breaking in my head
I burn the edges of the toast and call it what you said

And the kitchen glows like a distant shore
I keep setting places for someone who’s not here anymore
Every simmer, every stir feels like letting go
But the stove still remembers what I already know
It’s only me now, it’s only me now
In the soft white noise of a house too loud

I chop the onions and they blur into the air
Like every goodbye I never meant to wear
The fridge hums low like a prayer with no name
I write grocery lists I know won’t be the same
The recipe book falls open to your side
Stains on the page where your handwriting tried
To teach me patience in heat and time
Now I’m just following ghosts line by line

And the kitchen glows like a distant shore
I keep setting places for someone who’s not here anymore
Every simmer, every stir feels like letting go
But the stove still remembers what I already know
It’s only me now, it’s only me now
In the soft white noise of a house too loud

If I cook slow enough maybe you return in steam
In the curl of broth like a half-remembered dream
But the pan just sings its electric sigh
And I learn goodbye is something you don’t fry

And the kitchen glows like a distant shore
No one comes in when I open the door
Every bite tastes like learning how to know
That love doesn’t leave, it just changes the show
It’s only me now, it’s only me now
And I’m learning the shape of a life somehow

songs.subject: a reggaeton song about a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
En la esquina suena el clip, clip, clip,
la navaja baila como un beat,
el barbero sabe lo que hay aquí,
cada cabeza trae un secreto pa’ decir.
Entra el alcalde con prisa y sudor,
sale más fresco pero carga presión,
el del colmado hablando de amor,
y el del delivery perdió el control.
Él no pregunta, pero ya lo ve,
todo el barrio se sienta a confesar,
entre espuma y el aftershave,
nadie puede nada ocultar.

Porque el barbero sabe todo el town,
todo lo que sube, todo lo que ha bajao’,
con un corte limpio y mirada de sound,
tiene al barrio entero bajo su crown.
Dime quién eres, siéntate aquí,
la silla gira y te hace sentir,
que en cada fade que te va a hacer,
tu vida entera va a revivir.

La señora dice que su hijo cambió,
pero el barbero ya lo notó,
entra callado, sale con flow,
pero en sus ojos hay otra voz.
Dos panas discuten de lealtad,
pero en la barbería hay verdad,
el espejo nunca va a mentir,
todo lo que eres se puede ver aquí.
Y cuando cae la noche en la city light,
el local aún quiere seguir alive,
porque el que entra no sale igual,
sale más real o quiere escapar.

Porque el barbero sabe todo el town,
todo lo que sube, todo lo que ha bajao’,
con un corte limpio y mirada de sound,
tiene al barrio entero bajo su crown.
Dime quién eres, siéntate aquí,
la silla gira y te hace sentir,
que en cada fade que te va a hacer,
tu vida entera va a revivir.

Tic tac, pasan las historias en la pared,
cada línea en su mano es un lazo de red,
no hay escape cuando te toca el clip,
el destino se sienta aquí.
Y él sonríe porque ya entendió,
que el barrio entero siempre confesó,
entre tijeras y un bajo profundo,
él es el testigo de todo su mundo.

songs.subject: a metal song about the one that got away
songs.lyrics:
I still hear your name in the static of rain
Like a signal that cuts through the midnight pain
We were fire in a cathedral of broken glass
Now I pray to the embers of a fading past
You left like a ghost with my heartbeat still in your hands
And I carved your shadow in shifting sand
Every road I take bends back to that day
When the world went silent and you walked away

You’re the one that got away in the thunder and flame
A beautiful wound I keep calling your name
I scream at the sky but it never replies
Just echoes of you in my fractured skies
You’re the one that got away, carved into bone
A love turned to iron, forever alone

I built my regret like a fortress of steel
Each memory a blade that I still feel
Your laughter haunts every amplifier scream
Like a corrupted frame in a dying dream
I wear your goodbye like a crown made of rust
A monument built from betrayal and trust
And every night when the silence gets loud
I see your face in the rolling crowd

You’re the one that got away in the thunder and flame
A beautiful wound I keep calling your name
I scream at the sky but it never replies
Just echoes of you in my fractured skies
You’re the one that got away, carved into bone
A love turned to iron, forever alone

If I could tear time from its stitched-up seams
I’d rewrite the ending, break all the machines
But the gears keep turning, they grind my soul
And your absence is the price of the role
So I stand in the ruins where we used to be whole
A survivor of love with a blackened soul
And I whisper your name to the void I became
Knowing you’ll never burn in my flame again

songs.subject: a celtic folk song about a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
In the shadow of the willows where the old men gather round,
On the worn and weathered benches set on cobblestone and ground,
There’s a quiet kind of legend where the pigeons wheel and call,
A man with folding chessboard who will wager kings and all.
He’s got a grin like a thundercloud rolling over Dublin Bay,
Speaks in riddles to the strangers who get tempted to play,
“Just a friendly little game now, nothing heavy, nothing grand,”
But he’s already rearranging all your hopes across the land.

Oh the park lights flicker, and the cold winds moan,
And the pawns march steady like they’ve got a mind of their own,
He’ll take your silver, he’ll take your pride,
Leave you staring at a check you never saw in time,
In the quiet of the evening where the lost bets lie,
The chess hustler is waiting and he’s always nearby.

Some say he learned from sailors on a long-forgotten pier,
From monks who carved out futures in the patterns they could hear,
Every opening a whisper, every sacrifice a tale,
Every fallen little bishop like a ship without a sail.
You think you’ve got him cornered, you’re a fox among the wheat,
But he’s smiling like a raven as he shifts you off your feet,
And the board becomes a stormfield where your certainty decays,
As he lures you ever deeper through the labyrinth of plays.

Oh the park lights flicker, and the cold winds moan,
And the pawns march steady like they’ve got a mind of their own,
He’ll take your silver, he’ll take your pride,
Leave you staring at a check you never saw in time,
In the quiet of the evening where the lost bets lie,
The chess hustler is waiting and he’s always nearby.

But sometimes when the night falls and the benches are all bare,
He sits alone with memories he never quite lays there,
For every king he’s toppled is a ghost he cannot name,
And every easy victory still flickers like a flame.
So if you hear the clatter of a board beneath the trees,
And a voice that sounds like laughter riding on the autumn breeze,
Best walk on by in silence, don’t be tempted, don’t delay,
For the game he’s always offering is never just a play.

Oh the park lights flicker, and the cold winds moan,
And the pawns march steady like they’ve got a mind of their own,
He’ll take your silver, he’ll take your pride,
Leave you staring at a check you never saw in time,
In the quiet of the evening where the lost bets lie,
The chess hustler is waiting and he’s always nearby.

songs.subject: a synthwave song about the last tree in a paved-over town
songs.lyrics:
Neon rain on the concrete veins
The city hums in electric chains
Steel horizons where green once grew
Just me and the echoes of what was true
I remember roots in the quiet ground
Now every memory is overlaid sound
Billboards flicker like dying stars
In a sky that forgot where the forests are

But there’s one last tree in a paved-over town
Standing where the world got broken down
Its leaves still catch what the night won’t say
In a language the machines can’t play
And I drive past lights that never sleep
To find the green in a world too deep
One last tree in a paved-over town
Refusing to fall, refusing to drown

Chrome reflections on empty streets
Where nature’s ghost still barely breathes
The wind is coded in broken signs
Repeating lost and forgotten lines
I trace the map where the rivers ran
Now it’s circuits drawn by a heavy hand
But somewhere under the static glow
Something ancient refuses to go

There’s one last tree in a paved-over town
Standing where the world got broken down
Its branches hum like a distant song
Of everything we did wrong
And I reach for green in a world of grey
Like a signal that won’t decay
One last tree in a paved-over town
Still reaching up, not backing down

If it falls, do we fall too?
Or just become what the silence knew?
A frozen loop in a neon sky
Where even the questions forget to die

One last tree in a paved-over town
A single pulse in the lost and found
Its roots go deeper than steel allows
Into the past that still endows
And I swear I hear it call my name
Through the static and the endless game
One last tree in a paved-over town
Still standing when the lights go down

songs.subject: a lo-fi hip hop song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Dust on my boots, but the road feels new
Same old sky, but it don’t feel true
I left a part of me where the sirens sang
In the hollow night where the distant guns rang
Now I’m on a train with a paper bag
Folded up letters and a faded flag
Hands still shake when the doors slide wide
Like I’m not sure what I’m supposed to find

I’m coming home, but I don’t know me
A different ghost in a same old street
Heart still stuck in a far-off place
Time moves slow in a warm embrace
I’m coming home, but I don’t know how
To be the man I am right now

Streetlights flicker like a memory flash
Every loud sound pulls me back too fast
But the corner store still smells like rain
And nobody here says my name the same
I smile at strangers, I nod and wave
But I carry silence like a heavy weight
Trying to fit in my old skin again
While the war keeps talking inside my head

I’m coming home, but I don’t know me
A different ghost in a same old street
Heart still stuck in a far-off place
Time moves slow in a warm embrace
I’m coming home, but I don’t know how
To be the man I am right now

If I close my eyes, I can almost sleep
Where the world was loud and the nights were deep
But peace is a sound I forgot to trust
Like a sky that forgot how to rain on us
So I breathe it in, let the quiet stay
Try to learn what I can’t erase away
No more orders, no more fight to prove
Just a broken rhythm trying to move

I’m coming home, but I don’t know me
A different ghost in a same old street
Heart still stuck in a far-off place
Time moves slow in a warm embrace
I’m coming home, but I don’t know how
To be the man I am right now
songs.lyrics_en:
Dust on my boots, but the road feels new
Same old sky, but it don’t feel true
I left a part of me where the sirens sang
In the hollow night where the distant guns rang
Now I’m on a train with a paper bag
Folded up letters and a faded flag
Hands still shake when the doors slide wide
Like I’m not sure what I’m supposed to find

I’m coming home, but I don’t know me
A different ghost in a same old street
Heart still stuck in a far-off place
Time moves slow in a warm embrace
I’m coming home, but I don’t know how
To be the man I am right now

Streetlights flicker like a memory flash
Every loud sound pulls me back too fast
But the corner store still smells like rain
And nobody here says my name the same
I smile at strangers, I nod and wave
But I carry silence like a heavy weight
Trying to fit in my old skin again
While the war keeps talking inside my head

I’m coming home, but I don’t know me
A different ghost in a same old street
Heart still stuck in a far-off place
Time moves slow in a warm embrace
I’m coming home, but I don’t know how
To be the man I am right now

Heart still stuck in a far-off place
Time moves slow in a warm embrace
I’m coming home, but I don’t know how
To be the man I am right now

songs.subject: a bluegrass song about the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The tents are swayin’ in the summer breeze,
Lantern light in the sycamore trees,
Fiddles hummin’ like a memory song,
But the last night’s comin’ on too strong.
Dust on boots and a worn-out grin,
Friends we made we may not see again,
Somebody’s laughin’ by the fire’s glow,
Tryin’ to hold on before we go.

Oh, the last night of the festival,
Where the hearts run wild and the lights feel small,
We dance like we’re never gonna fall,
But the morning’s gonna steal it all.
So sing it loud, let the banjo ring,
Hold onto everything this night can bring,
‘Cause when the sky turns pale and cold,
All we’ll have is the stories told.

The old stage creaks with a final tune,
Moon hangs low like a tired spoon,
Couples spin on the trampled ground,
Every goodbye’s a hollow sound.
Vendor stalls all shuttered tight,
Paper stars still catch the light,
And every step feels just a little slow,
Like nobody really wants to go.

Oh, the last night of the festival,
Where the hearts run wild and the lights feel small,
We dance like we’re never gonna fall,
But the morning’s gonna steal it all.
So sing it loud, let the banjo ring,
Hold onto everything this night can bring,
‘Cause when the sky turns pale and cold,
All we’ll have is the stories told.

If I close my eyes I can still believe,
This kind of magic don’t ever leave,
But the fire burns down, the embers fade,
And even the brightest plans get made to break.

Oh, the last night of the festival,
Where the hearts run wild and the lights feel small,
We dance like we’re never gonna fall,
But the morning’s gonna steal it all.
So sing it loud, let the banjo ring,
Hold onto everything this night can bring,
‘Cause when the sky turns pale and cold,
All we’ll have is the stories told.

songs.subject: a folk song about a long-haul trucker calling home at 3am
songs.lyrics:
The highway hums like a tired old song
Headlights flicker, the night runs long
Coffee gone cold in a paper cup
I count the miles till the sun comes up

Three a.m. and the world’s asleep
But I’m chasing lines that I have to keep
Diesel rhythm, steady and slow
Got nowhere else I need to go

So I call you home through the static line
In the dead of night when the stars don’t shine
Say I’m alright, just missing your light
From this rolling steel in the dark of night
Hold on, love, till I’m back again
When the road lets go and the wheels don’t spin

Your voice comes soft like a distant shore
Through a crackling sound in the speaker’s war
You ask me how the road has been
I say “the same” and you know what I mean

There’s a map in my hands, but it don’t feel real
Just painted dreams under spinning wheels
Every exit sign is a fading prayer
That I’ll find my way back there

So I call you home through the static line
In the dead of night when the stars don’t shine
Say I’m alright, just missing your light
From this rolling steel in the dark of night
Hold on, love, till I’m back again
When the road lets go and the wheels don’t spin

And I swear I can hear your breath in the wire
Like a candle burning against the tire
If love is a place then I’m almost there
Even when I’m lost in the thinning air

So I call you home through the static line
In the dead of night when the stars don’t shine
Say I’m alright, just missing your light
From this rolling steel in the dark of night
Hold on, love, till I’m back again
When the road lets go and the wheels don’t spin

songs.subject: a doo-wop song about studying alone at two in the morning
songs.lyrics:
Two in the morning, just me and the glow
Desk lamp humming soft and low
Books are open, but my mind drifts slow
Tryna keep up, but it’s hard to know
Pages turning like a quiet breeze
Coffee cooling at my knees
Outside the world is fast asleep
But I’ve got promises I gotta keep

Shooby doo-wah, I’m learning alone
Shooby doo-wah, in this quiet zone
Tick tock to the rhythm of the wall clock tone
Shooby doo-wah, I’m on my own
But I keep going, yeah I don’t stop
Even when my eyelids drop
Shooby doo-wah, I’m holding on
Till the early light of dawn

Highlight lines in a fading ink
Try to focus, try to think
Every answer on the edge of a blink
Feels like I’m standing at the brink
Notes in margins, messy and wide
Questions piling up inside
But there’s a fire I can’t deny
Even when I’m half asleep inside

Shooby doo-wah, I’m learning alone
Shooby doo-wah, in this quiet zone
Tick tock to the rhythm of the wall clock tone
Shooby doo-wah, I’m on my own
Still I keep going, yeah I don’t stop
Even when my eyelids drop
Shooby doo-wah, I’m holding on
Till the early light of dawn

Maybe the night is heavy, but it’s mine to use
Every little page is a way I choose
In the silence I hear my thoughts collide
Like a doo-wop echo in the night inside

Shooby doo-wah, I’m learning alone
Shooby doo-wah, in this quiet zone
But I’m getting closer, line by line
Shooby doo-wah, I’ll be just fine
When the morning breaks and the night is gone
I’ll still be singing this same old song
Shooby doo-wah, I made it through
Two in the morning, me and the truth
