songs.subject: a afrobeats song about the smell of a grandmother's kitchen
songs.lyrics:
Morning light through the wooden door
Smoke dancing low on the kitchen floor
I hear the pot sing, soft and slow
Like stories only grandmama know
Pepper and thyme in the air so thick
Palm oil glowing in a cast iron mix
Every corner got a memory
That’s where I learned what home could be

It smells like love in grandmama’s kitchen
Where every wound start slowly mending
Oh that fire, it keeps on giving
In grandmama’s kitchen, I’m living
It smells like love, it smells like home
Even when I’m far, it calls me home
Grandmama’s kitchen, I’m never alone
That smell stays with me, it follows me on

Grinding spices in the mortar stone
She hums a tune that I’ve always known
Yam on the fire, stew in the pot
Her hands move like they never forgot
Every recipe is a prayer she speaks
Every meal is a love she keeps
And when the steam rise up so high
I swear I can touch the sky

It smells like love in grandmama’s kitchen
Where every wound start slowly mending
Oh that fire, it keeps on giving
In grandmama’s kitchen, I’m living
It smells like love, it smells like home
Even when I’m far, it calls me home
Grandmama’s kitchen, I’m never alone
That smell stays with me, it follows me on

When the world gets loud and I lose my way
I close my eyes and I drift away
Back to that stove, back to that flame
Back where even sorrow knows my name
One deep breath and I feel alright
Like she still cooking through the night
Love so strong it don’t fade away
It just gets sweeter every day

It smells like love in grandmama’s kitchen
Where every wound start slowly mending
Oh that fire, it keeps on giving
In grandmama’s kitchen, I’m living
It smells like love, it smells like home
Even when I’m far, it calls me home
Grandmama’s kitchen, I’m never alone
That smell stays with me, it follows me on

songs.subject: a drum and bass song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Streetlights flicker like memories I left behind
Boots still echo in the corners of my mind
Dust on my jacket, salt in my breath
I learned how to live when I learned about death
Now every shadow feels a little too loud
Like thunder still rolling beneath every cloud
I count the distance from where I was before
To the quiet step of my hand on the door

I’m coming home, I’m coming home
Through broken roads and over stone
I’ve carried fire, I’ve carried rain
I’ve carried names I can’t explain
But I’m alive, and I still roam
So let the night sing me home
I’m coming home, I’m coming home
Back to where I don’t stand alone

Train car humming like a heartbeat gone soft
Maps in my pocket all folded and lost
Faces I knew are now fading to gray
Like radio signals that drifted away
Every mile feels like shedding a chain
Learning the silence that follows the pain
And I don’t know who I’m supposed to be
A stranger returning, or finally me

I’m coming home, I’m coming home
Through broken roads and over stone
I’ve carried fire, I’ve carried rain
I’ve carried names I can’t explain
But I’m alive, and I still roam
So let the night sing me home
I’m coming home, I’m coming home
Back to where I don’t stand alone

If the past still knocks at my door in the dark
I’ll leave it outside where it can’t leave a mark
I’m learning that peace is a hard thing to trust
But I’m laying down all that I turned into dust
Let the bassline shake what I can’t undo
Let the silence wash me clean and true
I’m not who I was in the smoke and the flame
But I still remember my own name

I’m coming home, I’m coming home
Through broken roads and over stone
I’ve carried fire, I’ve carried rain
I’ve carried names I can’t explain
But I’m alive, and I still roam
So let the night sing me home
I’m coming home, I’m coming home
Back to where I don’t stand alone

songs.subject: a jazz song about a barber who knows the whole town
songs.lyrics:
Morning light on a chipped old mirror glow
He sharpens up the clippers like he’s starting up a show
Every head that walks in carries secrets in the air
And he trims a little silence while pretending not to care

He’s got names written deeper than the lines upon his face
Knows who’s leaving, who’s returning, who is stuck in one place
From the banker to the drummer with a suitcase full of rain
He hears the city breathing through the hum inside the blades

Oh, the barber knows the whole town by heart
Every laugh, every lie, every breaking part
In the sway of the chair and the scissors’ tune
He’s a midnight confessor in a sunlit room
Yeah, the barber knows the whole town by name
And nobody ever leaves quite the same

He’s got gossip like a record spinning slow and sweet
But he never plays it louder than the rhythm of the street
If you’re falling, he’ll just taper off the edge you cannot see
And leave you looking sharper than the man you used to be

Oh, the barber knows the whole town by heart
Every laugh, every lie, every breaking part
In the sway of the chair and the scissors’ tune
He’s a midnight confessor in a sunlit room
Yeah, the barber knows the whole town by name
And nobody ever leaves quite the same

And when the day fades into whiskey-colored dusk
He sweeps up all the stories nobody wants to trust
But in the mirror’s hush, he smiles at what he knows
‘Cause every head he’s held has helped the whole town grow

Oh, the barber knows the whole town by heart
Every laugh, every lie, every breaking part
In the sway of the chair and the scissors’ tune
He’s a midnight confessor in a sunlit room
Yeah, the barber knows the whole town by name
And nobody ever leaves quite the same

songs.subject: a lo-fi hip hop song about a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
Morning light on the sewing machine glow
Block’s still half asleep but I’m already low and slow
Thread in the needle like a bassline loop
Fixing up dreams in a faded denim suit
Every hem I drop got a story to tell
From the corner laundromat to the stairwell smell
Neighbors come by with a rip and a tear
I turn worn-out cloth into something they can wear

I dress the whole block in the quiet and the calm
Stitching up the cracks like a healing balm
One seam, one dream, in the soft street hum
Where the broken things go when the day is done
Yeah, I dress the whole block in the slow-time glow
Where the city breathes in and the worries go
Needle and thread like a lullaby song
Holding us together when the nights run long

Old man coat with the elbow gone thin
Kids’ school uniforms with patches tucked in
Bride’s veil waiting on a dusty rack
And a jacket from a son who ain’t coming back
I measure out hope in a crooked chalk line
Turn sorrow to style one stitch at a time
Radio plays that lo-fi static sound
While I keep the rhythm of the fabric going round

I dress the whole block in the quiet and the calm
Stitching up the cracks like a healing balm
One seam, one dream, in the soft street hum
Where the broken things go when the day is done
Yeah, I dress the whole block in the slow-time glow
Where the city breathes in and the worries go
Needle and thread like a lullaby song
Holding us together when the nights run long

If the world comes loose at the edge of the sky
I’ll be right here with a steady eye
Every fray gets a second chance
In the quiet motion of my hands
No rush, no race, just the fabric of time
Turning worn-out hearts into something fine

I dress the whole block in the quiet and the calm
Stitching up the cracks like a healing balm
One seam, one dream, in the soft street hum
Where the broken things go when the day is done

Where the city breathes in and the worries go
Needle and thread like a lullaby song
Holding us together when the nights run long

songs.subject: a bossa nova song about a lighthouse keeper's love letters to the sea
songs.lyrics:
I wake before the harbor learns the color of the day
The lighthouse hums a lonely tune where gulls have flown away
Your silver breath is on the glass, your fingerprints on stone
I write you in the language that the tide has always known

Ink slips like moonlight through my hands, it trembles when I try
To tell you how the wind still calls your name across the sky
Each envelope a drifting shell I send beyond the foam
Addressed to every wave that ever carried sailors home

Oh sea, my endless listener, my lover dressed in blue
I fold my heart in paper boats and let them drift to you
If distance is a shoreline, then I’m never far apart
For every wave returning is your answer to my heart

The lantern turns like memory that circles through the night
It writes your name in golden arcs that stitch the dark with light
I trace your voice in currents where the deep and quiet meet
And hear you in the hollow of the wind along the street

Oh sea, my endless listener, my lover dressed in blue
I fold my heart in paper boats and let them drift to you
If distance is a shoreline, then I’m never far apart
For every wave returning is your answer to my heart

And when the storm unbuttons all the secrets from the shore
I read your scattered letters in the thunder’s restless roar
You say you are not leaving, you are never far away
Just learning how to speak through every tide that breaks the day

Oh sea, my endless listener, my lover dressed in blue
I fold my heart in paper boats and let them drift to you
If distance is a shoreline, then I’m never far apart
For every wave returning is your answer to my heart

songs.subject: a reggae song about texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
Midnight glow on my phone screen light
Your name pop up and it don’t feel right
I said I’d be done, I said I’d be strong
But one little text and I fall back wrong
Typing “hey” like it’s nothing at all
But I know that’s how I start to fall
Promises I made start slipping away
In the quiet of the night I sway

I shouldn’t text you, but I do
Caught in the habit, stuck on you
Every word just pulls me in
Back to where I shouldn’t begin
I shouldn’t want you, but I do
Reggae rhythm in my confused truth
Hit send, then I regret it too
I shouldn’t text you, but I do

Blue ticks showing you already read
Now I’m overthinking every word I said
Was it too much, was it too fast
Chasing a feeling that was never meant to last
Phone on silent but my heart loud
Lost in a message I shouldn’t be proud
I try to delete it, I try to resist
But you’ve got a hold in a digital twist

I shouldn’t text you, but I do
Caught in the habit, stuck on you
Every word just pulls me in
Back to where I shouldn’t begin
I shouldn’t want you, but I do
Reggae rhythm in my confused truth
Hit send, then I regret it too
I shouldn’t text you, but I do

Maybe it’s the night, maybe it’s the beat
Maybe it’s the way your name repeats
I know better but I still ignore
Every warning I’ve felt before
One more message, then I swear I’m done
But I’ve said that line since the first one

I shouldn’t text you, but I do
Caught in the habit, stuck on you
Every word just pulls me in
Back to where I shouldn’t begin
I shouldn’t want you, but I do
Reggae rhythm in my confused truth
Hit send, then I regret it too
I shouldn’t text you, but I do

songs.subject: a reggae song about the last night of a festival
songs.lyrics:
The sun go down on the final day
Lantern light start to drift away
Barefoot steps on a dusty ground
Hear the echo of the last drum sound
Smoke in the air, sweet mango fire
Voices rise but they’re getting tired
One more song for the open sky
Before we all say our goodbyes

Oh, it’s the last night of the festival
Dancing like we don’t want to let it go
Hold it tight in the rhythm we found
Till the morning comes and breaks it down
Oh, it’s the last night of the festival
Every heart beating irie and slow
Sing it loud till the lights run low
In the glow where the good vibes flow

Flags still waving but the wind is gone
People smiling like they’ve held on long
Strangers here but we feel the same
Carved in laughter, we remember names
Steel drums fading like a distant tide
Stars are waking on the other side
We move together in a final groove
Nothing left we have to prove

Oh, it’s the last night of the festival
Dancing like we don’t want to let it go
Hold it tight in the rhythm we found
Till the morning comes and breaks it down
Oh, it’s the last night of the festival
Every heart beating irie and slow
Sing it loud till the lights run low
In the glow where the good vibes flow

When the speakers fade and the crowd gets thin
We’ll carry the echo deep within
No ending can erase this flame
Every soul here knows the name
Of a night that turned the dark to gold
A story that we’ll always hold

Oh, it’s the last night of the festival
Dancing like we don’t want to let it go
Hold it tight in the rhythm we found
Till the morning comes and breaks it down
Oh, it’s the last night of the festival
Every heart beating irie and slow
Sing it loud till the lights run low
In the glow where the good vibes flow

songs.subject: a pop song about a getaway driver's last job
songs.lyrics:
Keys in my hand like a prayer I don’t believe
Neon bleeding on the glass of empty streets
One last call on a burner in my coat
“You ready?” yeah, but I don’t say a note
Engine hums like a secret I can’t keep
Every mile feels like it’s cutting into me
I learned the exits like scars on my mind
But tonight I’m running out of time

One last job, then I’m gone from the night
Burning rubber till the morning hits the light
If I don’t look back, I might just be free
But the past rides shotgun next to me
One last job, hear the sirens sing
Steel and smoke on a wire too thin
If I make it out, I swear I’ll change
But nothing ever leaves this game

Rearview mirror got a ghost in its frame
Every face I left blurred out with no name
Cash in the trunk like a weight on my chest
Feels heavier than all the rest
Corner hits, tires scream on the bend
I know the rules but I bend them again
Every shortcut tastes like borrowed sin
And I don’t know where I end or begin

One last job, then I’m gone from the night
Burning rubber till the morning hits the light
If I don’t look back, I might just be free
But the past rides shotgun next to me
One last job, hear the sirens sing
Steel and smoke on a wire too thin
If I make it out, I swear I’ll change
But nothing ever leaves this game

Headlights strobing like a warning in my veins
Every second here feels like stolen remains
I see the exit glowing up ahead
But I hear every word I never said
If I disappear in the static and flame
Tell them I was never good at my name
Just a shadow trying to outrun the dawn
Knowing all along I was already gone

One last job, now I’m gone from the night
Fading out where the road meets the light
If I don’t look back, I might just be free
And the past can’t ride shotgun with me
One last job, hear the sirens sing
Steel and smoke on a wire too thin
If I make it out, I swear I’ll change
But nothing ever leaves this game

songs.subject: a indie rock song about a chess hustler in the park
songs.lyrics:
He sets up his kingdom on a cracked green bench
Kings made of plastic, queens that never flinch
Coffee in a paper cup, smoke in the cold
He reads every passerby like a story half-told
They come from the subway with their time to kill
Thinking it’s just a game, thinking it’s all skill
But he’s been here longer than the paint on the park
Counting every hesitation, every flicker in the dark

And he says “play me, play me, if you think you’re so bright”
But nobody leaves here feeling like they won the fight
It’s pawns for your pride, it’s rooks for your doubt
In the chess hustler’s park, there’s no easy way out
No easy way out

A kid in a hoodie tries to bluff his way through
Moves like he’s seen the world but it’s mostly untrue
The hustler just smiles like he’s heard it before
Like a thousand broken egos scattered across the board
Leaves start to gather like verdicts at feet
Every checkmate lands like a familiar defeat
And the trees lean closer like they’re keeping score
Of who walked in proud and who walked out unsure

And he says “play me, play me, if you think you’re so bright”
But nobody leaves here feeling like they won the fight
It’s knights in the shadows, it’s bishops in doubt
In the chess hustler’s park, there’s no easy way out
No easy way out

But late in the evening when the lamps start to hum
He stares at the empty squares, thinks where he’s from
Was he born a dealer of defeat and pride?
Or just another king with nowhere to hide?
The wind moves the pieces like it knows his name
Whispers that winning is a different kind of game
And for a moment he wonders if anyone sees
The man behind the wagers, the rust in the breeze

And he says “play me, play me, if you think you’re so bright”
But the park goes quiet as it swallows the light
It’s kings made of nothing and truth carved out
In the chess hustler’s park, there’s no easy way out
No easy way out

songs.subject: a R&B song about a tailor who dresses the whole block
songs.lyrics:
He wakes up when the city still yawns in the dark
Needle and thread where he learned how to make his mark
Corner shop humming like a low sweet song
Every stitch he lays down telling folks they belong
Denim dreams and Sunday suits in the window light
He can turn a hard week into something that feels right
Kids come in with the holes in their story and clothes
He don’t ask too many questions, he already knows

He’s the tailor on the block, got the whole street fly
From the low-rise jeans to the church-day tie
If you need a new life, he can cut it just right
Make you feel brand new by the end of the night
Yeah, he’s sewing up hope in every seam that he drops
Everybody looking fresh when they step out his shop
He don’t just fix fabric, he lifts up the flock
That’s the love that you get from the tailor on the block

Mama coming in with her coat worn thin at the sleeve
He says “give me a minute, I got something you’ll believe”
Turns old into gold with a flick of his hand
Like he’s weaving up futures from the scraps in the land
He remembers every fit, every face, every name
Every zipper he fixes feels like changing the game
And the block starts shining when they wear what he made
Like the sun came down early just to bless the parade

He’s the tailor on the block, got the whole street fly
From the low-rise jeans to the church-day tie
If you need a new life, he can cut it just right
Make you feel brand new by the end of the night
Yeah, he’s sewing up hope in every seam that he drops
Everybody looking fresh when they step out his shop
He don’t just fix fabric, he lifts up the flock
That’s the love that you get from the tailor on the block

And when the world feels too big and your edges come loose
He’s the calm in the chaos, the thread pulling you through
One stitch at a time, he reminds you you’re seen
In the fabric of the block, you’re part of the dream

He’s the tailor on the block, got the whole street fly
From the low-rise jeans to the church-day tie
If you need a new life, he can cut it just right

That’s the love that you get from the tailor on the block

songs.subject: a funk song about coming home from the war
songs.lyrics:
Dust on my boots, and a thousand-yard stare
Train whistle crying like it knows what I bear
Pocket full of letters I never replied
Now I’m counting streetlights as I ride back inside

Uniform heavy but it don’t feel like mine
Like I borrowed a body from a different timeline
Every mile marker’s a ghost in my mind
But I’m closer to home with each click of the line

I’m coming home, coming home, let the rhythm take me down
Back to the bassline heartbeat of this sleeping little town
I’ve been far, I’ve been gone, but the night is letting go
And I’m stepping off the war and into funk I used to know
Yeah I’m coming home, coming home, let the horns call out my name
Where the hurt turns into music and the silence turns to flame
I’ve been gone too long to stay a shadow in the chrome
So I’m riding that groove all the way back home

Mama on the porch with a lantern in her hand
Like she’s been holding time the best she can stand
And I don’t know what version of me she will see
But she smiles like she still believes in me

There’s a crack in the sidewalk where I left my youth
Now I’m walking on truth that don’t feel like truth
Every step is a drumbeat, every breath is a song
But I gotta learn where my heart still belongs

I’m coming home, coming home, let the rhythm take me down
Back to the bassline heartbeat of this sleeping little town
I’ve been far, I’ve been gone, but the night is letting go
And I’m stepping off the war and into funk I used to know
Yeah I’m coming home, coming home, let the horns call out my name
Where the hurt turns into music and the silence turns to flame
I’ve been gone too long to stay a shadow in the chrome
So I’m riding that groove all the way back home

And the silence hits different when the guns ain’t in your ear
It’s loud in a way that makes you feel everything you fear
But the band keeps playing like a prayer through the dark
Turning every broken memory into a spark

I’m coming home, coming home, let the rhythm take me down
Back to the bassline heartbeat of this sleeping little town
I’ve been far, I’ve been gone, but the night is letting go
And I’m stepping off the war and into funk I used to know
Yeah I’m coming home, coming home, let the horns call out my name
Where the hurt turns into music and the silence turns to flame
I’ve been gone too long to stay a shadow in the chrome
So I’m riding that groove all the way back home

songs.subject: a swing song about falling for someone from your hometown
songs.lyrics:
I caught your laugh on a Sunday street,
Where the bakery air and the church bells meet,
Same old corners, new heartbeat,
When you turned around and looked at me.
Dust on the window of the corner store,
Where we swore we’d never want nothing more,
Now every step feels like a dance floor,
Since I saw you like never before.

Oh, I’m falling like a record spinning out of time,
Like a brass band swinging on a midnight line,
In the town where we grew up, yours and mine,
Now every little memory’s a valentine.
And I can’t pretend I don’t feel this way,
Like the hometown lights just learned to play,
Oh, I’m falling, falling in a swing parade,
For you in the place we were both made.

We know the cracks in every sidewalk stone,
Every shortcut path we ever roamed,
But I never knew I’d feel at home,
In the way your eyes made the ordinary glow.
Now the streetlights hum a different tune,
Like they’re nodding when I walk past you,
Even the wind seems to be in on it too,
Whispering secrets I never knew.

Oh, I’m falling like a record spinning out of time,
Like a brass band swinging on a midnight line,
In the town where we grew up, yours and mine,
Now every little memory’s a valentine.
And I can’t pretend I don’t feel this way,
Like the hometown lights just learned to play,
Oh, I’m falling, falling in a swing parade,
For you in the place we were both made.

Maybe it was always written in the cracks of this street,
Two old souls in familiar beat,
But now the rhythm won’t let me retreat,
Every step you take is a drum in me.
So take my hand when the evening calls,
Let’s rewrite what we thought were just old walls,
Turn this small-town silence into halls,
Where our story finally falls.

Oh, I’m falling like a record spinning out of time,
Like a brass band swinging on a midnight line,
In the town where we grew up, yours and mine,
Now every little memory’s a valentine.
And I can’t pretend I don’t feel this way,
Like the hometown lights just learned to play,
Oh, I’m falling, falling in a swing parade,
For you in the place we were both made.

When you turned around and looked at me.

Now every step feels like a dance floor,
Since I saw you like never before.

songs.subject: a post-rock song about a grandmother's hands
songs.lyrics:
Her hands were maps of winter light
Folded rivers in the morning white
I learned the world between her lines
Soft as dust in borrowed time
She held the kettle like a prayer
Steam wrote stories in the air
Every wrinkle, every seam
A place I once believed in

Oh, her hands, they carried whole seasons
Breaking bread with silent reasons
They knew the weight of letting go
And how to make the broken whole
Oh, her hands, they never trembled
Even when the years disassembled
Everything we thought would stay
They just kept shaping yesterday

She brushed the years from my sleeve
Like fallen leaves no one could grieve
And when she laughed, the room would bend
As if time forgot to end
There was a hymn in every touch
That said, “You do not need so much”
And I would listen, I would learn
While distant photographs would burn

Oh, her hands, they carried whole seasons
Breaking bread with silent reasons
They knew the weight of letting go
And how to make the broken whole
Oh, her hands, they never trembled
Even when the years disassembled
Everything we thought would stay
They just kept shaping yesterday

Now silence sits where warmth once grew
A chair that still remembers you
And I trace ghosts across the air
As if your fingers still are there
If I could speak the way you spoke
In touch, in light, in steady hope
I’d let the world fall from my grip
And learn again your fingertips

songs.subject: a swing song about texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
The clock ticks loud in my dim-lit room,
Got a glass of gin and a hint of doom,
Your name pops up like a loaded dice,
I told myself “don’t do this twice.”
But my thumbs start dancing on the screen,
Typing words I didn’t mean,
A little “hey” turns into flame,
And I swear I’m not the one to blame.

I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be,
Like a trumpet call in a smoky sea,
It swings and sways, it won’t let go,
That late-night rhythm saying “go, go, go.”
I know it’s wrong but it feels so right,
Like a jazz band playing through the night,
I’m caught in a melody wild and free—
I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be.

Your replies come fast, a syncopated beat,
Like tap shoes knocking down my feet,
We trade old jokes and borrowed sins,
And the whole night bends and spins.
The streetlights hum like a brassy horn,
I should’ve quit before it was born,
But every “lol” and every sign,
Pulls me deeper every line.

I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be,
Like a saxophone crying out at sea,
It swings too hard, it bends too low,
And I can’t find the way to say “no, no, no.”
I know it’s wrong but it feels so right,
Like a crowded club on a Friday night,
I’m lost in a rhythm I can’t unsee—
I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be.

Now the silence waits on the edge of dawn,
And I know I should’ve just left it alone,
But the last “you up?” still glows on my screen,
Like a spotlight cutting through a dream.
If I step away, will it fade away?
Or come back louder another day?
This swinging heart won’t sit still for me—
It keeps on texting what it shouldn’t be.

songs.subject: a ska song about making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Same cracked pavement, same chain-link sky
Same corner store where the hours go by
Streetlight flickers like it knows my name
Everybody here stuck in the same old game
But I got rhythm in my worn-out shoes
A one-way ticket and too much to lose
I hear a horn line cutting through the gray
Saying “move along, there’s a better way”

We’re breaking out of this dead-end block
Where the same four walls are all we’ve got
Step by step when the bassline drops
We don’t stop, we don’t stop, we don’t stop
From the backstreets to the open sun
We’re the ones who refuse to be done
Hear the skank of a brand new shock
We’re breaking out of this dead-end block

Bus stop dreams on a faded bench
Writing escape plans in a busted-up sketch
Friends all saying “that’s just talk”
But I’ve been counting every ticking clock
Found a downbeat in a radio song
Telling me I was never wrong
So I pack my doubts in a paper bag
And I follow the snare like a beating flag

We’re breaking out of this dead-end block
Where the same four walls are all we’ve got
Step by step when the bassline drops
We don’t stop, we don’t stop, we don’t stop
From the backstreets to the open sun
We’re the ones who refuse to be done
Hear the skank of a brand new shock
We’re breaking out of this dead-end block

And if the night tries to pull me under
I’ll turn it up like brass and thunder
Every setback is just a start
Every “no” is a spark in my heart
So I swing through the gates of doubt
Let the old life fade out, fade out

We’re breaking out of this dead-end block
Where the same four walls are all we’ve got
Step by step when the bassline drops
We don’t stop, we don’t stop, we don’t stop
From the backstreets to the open sun
We’re the ones who refuse to be done
Hear the skank of a brand new shock
We’re breaking out of this dead-end block

songs.subject: a klezmer song about texting someone you shouldn't
songs.lyrics:
In the glow of my phone at three in the night
The city’s asleep but my thumb is alight
I scroll through the names like a half-spun prayer
And stop at the one I should never dare
Your name in my screen like a mischievous tune
Like a fiddle that laughs at a wandering moon
I promise myself “no, not again”
Then I write “hey” and I press send

Oy, I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be texting
My heart is a bandoneon flexing and vexing
Every buzz is a drum, every read is a sting
And my better intentions just fly on a string
Oy, I’m laughing and crying, confused and obsessed
With a message I sent when I should have said “rest”
Now the night plays a klezmer inside of my chest
And I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be texting

The light from the screen paints my face pale blue
Like a candlelit tale that I can’t undo
You reply with a winking, half-hearted hello
And I’m already lost in the afterglow
The words start to dance like a drunken parade
Like clarinets weaving through choices I made
I type and I delete and I type once more
Like footsteps returning to a forbidden door

Oy, I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be texting
My judgment is slipping, my conscience is resting
Every vibration’s a slap and a kiss
In a carnival spun out of digital bliss
Oy, I’m laughing and crying, I’m lost in between
What I know that is wise and what flashes the screen
And the night keeps on playing that old violin scene
While I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be texting

If I put down the phone, will the silence grow loud?
Will I finally stand still, or dissolve in the crowd?
There’s a ghost in the glow and it won’t let me go
Like a tune from a street where I used to know
But the fiddle still calls through the wires and the glass
Saying “come, just one more, let the boundaries pass”
And I answer in echoes I cannot control
With a trembling thumb and a wandering soul

Oy, I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be texting
My heart is a question I keep on re-asking
Every word is a spin of the wheel and the flame
In a game where I never quite learn the name
Oy, I’m laughing and crying, I’m tangled, confessed
To a melody wrapped in my ribcage’s nest
And the night plays along like it knows all the rest
While I’m texting someone I shouldn’t be texting

songs.subject: a jazz song about looking back at your first open mic
songs.lyrics:
The sign-up sheet was coffee-stained and shaking in my hand
My name looked like a question I was trying to understand
The room was half-lit laughter, smoke curling in the air
And every empty table felt like someone judging there
I tuned my voice to courage, but it kept slipping out of key
My heart was a brushed snare drum rattling inside me
The host called out the numbers, like a doorbell to my fate
And I told myself “just breathe,” though I was already too late

And I remember that first open mic
Where the silence felt louder than the light
Every note a little stumble, every word a little tight
Still I sang like it could save my life
Oh I didn’t know what I didn’t know
But I let my trembling voice still go
And somehow in that fragile, falling flight
I became myself that night

The stage was barely bigger than the doubts beneath my shoes
The mic was like a mirror showing everything to lose
I laughed at my own timing, I forgot the second line
But the band just kept on walking like they’d known me all the time
There was a woman by the counter tapping rhythms with her glass
An old man closed his eyes like he was hearing his own past
And for a moment I was floating, not so terrified to fail
Just a melody of nerves in a slightly crooked scale

And I remember that first open mic
Where the silence felt louder than the light
Every note a little stumble, every word a little tight
Still I sang like it could save my life
Oh I didn’t know what I didn’t know
But I let my trembling voice still go
And somehow in that fragile, falling flight
I became myself that night

Now I walk into the room and I recognize that air
The same old wooden stage, the same uncertain prayer
But something in me softened where there used to be a fight
Like I learned to trust the dark between the notes and light

And I remember that first open mic
Like a photograph I hold inside
Every fear I thought would break me somehow turned out to be right
To bring me to the other side
Oh I didn’t know what I didn’t know
But I let my trembling voice still go
And in every room where strangers gather, still I find that night
I’m back at my first open mic

songs.subject: a jazz song about calling your mother after years of silence
songs.lyrics:
The phone light flickers in a quiet kitchen glow
Dust on the memories I never let you know
Your name sits heavy like a record on the shelf
Spinning in silence I’ve been keeping to myself
I walk the same streets where I learned to disappear
Every step an echo of the things I didn’t hear
Years like smoke between the truth and what I said
Now I’m dialing numbers that I swore were long dead

Ma, can you hear me through the static and the years?
It’s your child on the line with a voice full of tears
I don’t have the words but I’ve come so far to try
If you’re still out there, pick up tonight

The jazz bar’s humming like a secret I once knew
A trumpet bends the light the way regret can do
Every note is asking what I couldn’t understand
How love can slip away like sand through trembling hands
I practiced all my sentences, then threw them out again
Nothing sounds like truth when it’s been sharpened into pain
So I let the silence do the talking I can’t say
Hoping you still recognize the child who ran away

Ma, can you hear me through the static and the years?
It’s your child on the line with a voice full of tears
I don’t have the words but I’ve come so far to try
If you’re still out there, pick up tonight

And if the line goes empty, I’ll still leave my name
Like a candle in the window of a house that’s not the same
Forgiveness isn’t instant, it’s a slow and tender rain
Falling on the distance between love and all its pain
But I heard in every melody that drifts through smoky air
That even broken things can learn to say a prayer

Ma, can you hear me through the static and the years?
It’s your child on the line with a voice full of tears
I don’t have the words but I’ve come so far to try
If you’re still out there, pick up tonight

songs.subject: a R&B song about making it out of a dead-end block
songs.lyrics:
Same corner every night, same broken streetlight
Dreams feel heavy when you livin’ just to get by
Mama workin’ double shifts, still barely makin’ rent
I was dodgin’ every siren like it’s heaven-sent
Had friends turn ghosts in the blink of a season
Some lost to the system, some lost to their demons
I wrote plans on napkins, hoped they’d come true
But hope get quiet when the rent due

I’m tryna make it out that dead-end block
Where the clocks don’t move and the pressure don’t stop
Where they tell you “stay here” like it’s all you got
But I got more in my soul than this parking lot
Yeah, I’m tryna make it out, I’m tryna make it out
From the noise, from the doubt, from the same old route
If I fall, I get up, I won’t fade out
I’m tryna make it out that dead-end block

I learned survival before I learned peace
Before I knew love, I knew empty streets
Had big dreams tucked in my hoodie sleeve
While the world kept telling me what I couldn’t be
Every step felt like a gamble with fate
Every “no” just pushed me closer to escape
Now I see a skyline where the gray used to be
And I’m still that kid, just a little more free

I’m tryna make it out that dead-end block
Where the clocks don’t move and the pressure don’t stop
Where they tell you “stay here” like it’s all you got
But I got more in my soul than this parking lot
Yeah, I’m tryna make it out, I’m tryna make it out
From the noise, from the doubt, from the same old route
If I fall, I get up, I won’t fade out
I’m tryna make it out that dead-end block

And I still hear echoes of the life I could’ve had
If I never believed there was more than what I had
But I chose the long road even when it hurt
Now I wear every scar like a lesson learned
So if you see me shining, know it wasn’t luck
It was nights with no sleep when I still showed up
From the bottom where the silence always talks
Now I’m breaking every chain off that dead-end block

I’m tryna make it out that dead-end block
Where the clocks don’t move and the pressure don’t stop
Where they tell you “stay here” like it’s all you got
But I got more in my soul than this parking lot
Yeah, I’m tryna make it out, I’m tryna make it out
From the noise, from the doubt, from the same old route
If I fall, I get up, I won’t fade out
I’m tryna make it out that dead-end block

Where they tell you “stay here” like it’s all you got
But I got more in my soul than this parking lot
Yeah, I’m tryna make it out, I’m tryna make it out
From the noise, from the doubt, from the same old route
If I fall, I get up, I won’t fade out
I’m tryna make it out that dead-end block

I learned survival before I learned peace
Before I knew love, I knew empty streets
Had big dreams tucked in my hoodie sleeve
While the world kept telling me what I couldn’t be
Every step felt like a gamble with fate
Every “no” just pushed me closer to escape
Now I see a skyline where the gray used to be
And I’m still that kid, just a little more free

I’m tryna make it out that dead-end block
Where the clocks don’t move and the pressure don’t stop

And I still hear echoes of the life I could’ve had
If I never believed there was more than what I had
But I chose the long road even when it hurt
Now I wear every scar like a lesson learned
So if you see me shining, know it wasn’t luck
It was nights with no sleep when I still showed up
From the bottom where the silence always talks
Now I’m breaking every chain off that dead-end block

songs.subject: a hip hop song about the quiet after a fight with someone you love
songs.lyrics:
The room still hums where your words used to land
Like broken glass memories slipping through my hand
Clock ticks loud but we don’t say a thing
Just two silhouettes in the dust of what we bring
You on the couch, I’m pacing the floor
Both of us hurt but we don’t know what for
Echoes of anger still hanging in air
We act like we’re fine but we’re barely there
I replay the moment, every line we threw
Sharper than we meant, now cutting us through
You look at your phone like it’s safer to see
Than the face of the one who you used to believe

It’s the quiet after the fire burns out
Where love gets loud in a different kind of doubt
No doors slam, no more words to defend
Just the silence we don’t know how to mend
And I’m right here but it feels like a mile
Between your eyes and the ghost of your smile
We don’t break, we just don’t know how to stay
In the quiet after we push love away

You sigh like a question you won’t let me answer
Like hope got tired and lost its enhancer
I wanna reach out, but pride’s in the way
So we both stay stuck in the words we won’t say
Kitchen light flickers like it knows the mood
Cold coffee sitting where we used to be good
I hear your breath in the space between us
Heavy with truth neither one of us trusts
We built this home out of laughter and fire
Now every step feels like walking a wire
I miss how your voice used to call me back in
Now it just echoes where we don’t begin

It’s the quiet after the fire burns out
Where love gets loud in a different kind of doubt
No doors slam, no more words to defend
Just the silence we don’t know how to mend
And I’m right here but it feels like a mile
Between your eyes and the ghost of your smile
We don’t break, we just don’t know how to stay
In the quiet after we push love away

If silence could talk it would scream what we mean
That we’re both still here but stuck in between
The hurt and the healing, the now and the then
Wondering if we can start over again
I don’t need perfect, just need you to see
That the worst of this fight isn’t you or me
It’s the space that we let grow cold and wide
When neither of us wants to step to the other side

It’s the quiet after the fire burns out
Where love gets loud in a different kind of doubt
No doors slam, no more words to defend
Just the silence we don’t know how to mend
And I’m right here but it feels like a mile
Between your eyes and the ghost of your smile
We don’t break, we just don’t know how to stay
In the quiet after we push love away

songs.subject: a ska song about working two jobs and still falling behind
songs.lyrics:
Alarm clock screams at 5:09
Coffee’s cold but I say I’m fine
Punch the first clock, then I punch the next
Running on empty and over-stressed
Bus ride dreams of a better day
But the rent still laughs, it won’t go away
Check my pockets, just lint and doubt
But I keep moving, I can’t tap out

I’m working two jobs and still falling behind
Bills keep stacking in the back of my mind
Every paycheck’s gone before it arrives
Tell me how I’m supposed to survive
I’m spinning wheels but I’m stuck in place
Running this race at a losing pace
Working two jobs and still falling behind
Still falling behind

Boss says “smile,” I say “yes, sir”
Clock ticks louder than the blender’s blur
Second shift lights feel just like day
But my energy’s fading away
Try to save but it disappears
Broken hopes and unpaid years
Every dollar feels already spent
On promises I never meant

I’m working two jobs and still falling behind
Bills keep stacking in the back of my mind
Every paycheck’s gone before it arrives
Tell me how I’m supposed to survive
I’m spinning wheels but I’m stuck in place
Running this race at a losing pace
Working two jobs and still falling behind
Still falling behind

If I keep going, where does it end?
Just more hours I can’t defend
I need a break but I don’t know how
The future’s always “later” but I need it now
So I laugh it off, but it cuts so deep
Dreams I’m too tired to even keep

I’m working two jobs and still falling behind
Bills keep stacking in the back of my mind
Every paycheck’s gone before it arrives
Tell me how I’m supposed to survive
I’m spinning wheels but I’m stuck in place
Running this race at a losing pace
Working two jobs and still falling behind
Still falling behind
